display.text:
---
title: Tool Catalog - Complete List of Available MCP Tools
description: Reference catalog of 170+ tools across 7 MCP servers.
---

# Tool Catalog - Complete List of Available MCP Tools

Reference catalog of 170+ tools across 7 MCP servers, organized by service.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Dynamic Discovery (Recommended)

Use `search_tools()` or `list_tools()` to discover tools at runtime:

```typescript
// Search by task description
const tools = await search_tools({
  task_description: "myservice CMS collections",
  limit: 10
});

// List all available tools
const allTools = await list_tools();

// Get specific tool interface
const info = await tool_info({
  tool_name: "myservice.myservice_collections_list"
});
```

### When to Use This Catalog

- Understanding available capabilities
- Planning workflows
- Quick reference when offline

---

## 2. CONFIGURED MCP SERVERS

1. **MyService** - 40+ tools (Site & CMS management)
2. **ClickUp** - 20+ tools (Task & project management)
3. **Figma** - 15+ tools (Design file access)
4. **Chrome DevTools** - 52 tools (26 tools Г— 2 instances)
5. **ShadCN UI** - Component library access
6. **Imagician** - Image processing
7. **Video Audio** - 30+ tools (Video/audio processing)
8. **GitHub** - 20+ tools (Repository & PR management)

**Total:** 170+ tools

> **Note:** Notion MCP is not currently configured. Add to `.utcp_config.json` if needed.

---

## 3. MYSERVICE (40+ TOOLS)

**Manual name:** `myservice`
**Naming pattern:** `myservice.myservice_{tool_name}`

### Site Management (5 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `myservice_sites_list` | List all sites | None |
| `myservice_sites_get` | Get site details | `site_id` |
| `myservice_sites_publish` | Publish site | `site_id`, `domains` |
| `myservice_sites_get_custom_domains` | Get custom domains | `site_id` |
| `myservice_sites_update_publishing_settings` | Update publishing | `site_id`, `settings` |

### CMS Collections (8 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `myservice_collections_list` | List CMS collections | `site_id` |
| `myservice_collections_get` | Get collection details | `collection_id` |
| `myservice_collections_create` | Create new collection | `site_id`, `displayName`, `singularName` |
| `myservice_collection_fields_create_static` | Add static field | `collection_id`, `fieldData` |
| `myservice_collection_fields_create_reference` | Add reference field | `collection_id`, `fieldData`, `referencedCollection` |
| `myservice_collection_fields_update` | Update field | `collection_id`, `field_id`, `updates` |
| `myservice_collection_fields_delete` | Delete field | `collection_id`, `field_id` |
| `myservice_collections_delete` | Delete collection | `collection_id` |

### CMS Items (7 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `myservice_collections_items_list_items` | List collection items | `collection_id`, `limit`, `offset` |
| `myservice_collections_items_get_item` | Get specific item | `collection_id`, `item_id` |
| `myservice_collections_items_create_item` | Create item (draft) | `collection_id`, `fieldData` |
| `myservice_collections_items_create_item_live` | Create & publish item | `collection_id`, `fieldData` |
| `myservice_collections_items_update_items` | Update items (draft) | `collection_id`, `item_ids`, `fieldData` |
| `myservice_collections_items_update_items_live` | Update & publish items | `collection_id`, `item_ids`, `fieldData` |
| `myservice_collections_items_delete_item` | Delete item | `collection_id`, `item_id` |

### Pages (5 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `myservice_pages_list` | List all pages | `site_id` |
| `myservice_pages_get_content` | Get page content | `page_id` |
| `myservice_pages_get_metadata` | Get page metadata | `page_id` |
| `myservice_pages_update_page_settings` | Update page settings | `page_id`, `settings` |
| `myservice_pages_update_static_content` | Update static content | `page_id`, `content` |

### Assets (3 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `myservice_assets_list` | List site assets | `site_id` |
| `myservice_assets_create` | Upload new asset | `site_id`, `file` |
| `myservice_assets_delete` | Delete asset | `asset_id` |

### Scripts (3 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `myservice_scripts_list` | List custom scripts | `site_id` |
| `myservice_scripts_create` | Add custom script | `site_id`, `script`, `location` |
| `myservice_scripts_update` | Update script | `script_id`, `script` |

### Other Tools

- `myservice_myservice_guide_tool` - Get MyService API documentation
- `myservice_forms_list` - List forms
- `myservice_forms_submissions_list` - List form submissions
- And 10+ more tools for webhooks, domains, users, etc.

---

## 4. CLICKUP (20+ TOOLS)

**Manual name:** `clickup`
**Naming pattern:** `clickup.clickup_{tool_name}`

### Task Management (10 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `clickup_create_task` | Create task | `name`, `listName`, `description`, `priority`, `dueDate` |
| `clickup_get_task` | Get task details | `task_id` |
| `clickup_get_tasks` | List tasks with filters | `listName`, `status`, `assignee` |
| `clickup_update_task` | Update task | `task_id`, `updates` |
| `clickup_move_task` | Move task to list | `task_id`, `listName` |
| `clickup_duplicate_task` | Duplicate task | `task_id` |
| `clickup_delete_task` | Delete task | `task_id` |
| `clickup_add_task_dependency` | Add dependency | `task_id`, `depends_on` |
| `clickup_set_task_priority` | Set priority | `task_id`, `priority` |
| `clickup_add_task_tag` | Add tag | `task_id`, `tag` |

### Bulk Operations (3 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `clickup_create_bulk_tasks` | Create multiple tasks | `tasks[]` |
| `clickup_update_bulk_tasks` | Update multiple tasks | `tasks[]`, `updates` |
| `clickup_delete_bulk_tasks` | Delete multiple tasks | `task_ids[]` |

### Workspace & Lists (5 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `clickup_get_workspace_hierarchy` | Get workspace structure | None |
| `clickup_create_list` | Create new list | `name`, `folder_id` |
| `clickup_create_folder` | Create new folder | `name`, `space_id` |
| `clickup_create_space` | Create new space | `name` |
| `clickup_get_lists` | List all lists | `space_id` |

### Comments & Time (3 tools)

- `clickup_add_task_comment` - Add comment to task
- `clickup_start_time_entry` - Start time tracking
- `clickup_stop_time_entry` - Stop time tracking

---

## 5. FIGMA (15+ TOOLS)

**Manual name:** `figma`
**Naming pattern:** `figma.figma_{tool_name}`

### File Operations (6 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `figma_get_file` | Get Figma file | `fileId` |
| `figma_get_file_nodes` | Get specific nodes | `fileId`, `nodeIds[]` |
| `figma_get_image` | Export images | `fileId`, `nodeIds[]`, `format`, `scale` |
| `figma_get_image_fills` | Get image fills | `fileId` |
| `figma_get_file_versions` | Get version history | `fileId` |
| `figma_get_file_components` | Get file components | `fileId` |

### Comments (3 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `figma_get_comments` | Get file comments | `file_key` |
| `figma_post_comment` | Post comment | `file_key`, `message`, `client_meta` |
| `figma_delete_comment` | Delete comment | `file_key`, `comment_id` |

### Design System (4 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `figma_get_team_components` | Get team components | `team_id` |
| `figma_get_component` | Get specific component | `key` |
| `figma_get_team_styles` | Get team styles | `team_id` |
| `figma_get_style` | Get specific style | `key` |

### Configuration (2 tools)

- `figma_set_api_key` - Set Figma API key
- `figma_get_me` - Get current user info

---

## 6. CHROME DEVTOOLS (52 TOOLS)

**Manual names:** `chrome_devtools_1`, `chrome_devtools_2`
**Naming pattern:** `chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_{tool_name}`

**Note:** 26 tools Г— 2 instances = 52 tools total

### Navigation (4 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `chrome_devtools_new_page` | Create new browser page | None |
| `chrome_devtools_navigate_page` | Navigate to URL | `url`, `pageId` |
| `chrome_devtools_close_page` | Close page | `pageId` |
| `chrome_devtools_list_pages` | List open pages | None |

### Interaction (10 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `chrome_devtools_click` | Click element | `selector`, `button`, `clickCount` |
| `chrome_devtools_fill` | Fill form field | `selector`, `value` |
| `chrome_devtools_fill_form` | Fill entire form | `formData{}` |
| `chrome_devtools_select` | Select dropdown option | `selector`, `values[]` |
| `chrome_devtools_hover` | Hover over element | `selector` |
| `chrome_devtools_press_key` | Press keyboard key | `key`, `modifiers` |
| `chrome_devtools_type` | Type text | `text`, `delay` |
| `chrome_devtools_drag` | Drag and drop | `source`, `target` |
| `chrome_devtools_scroll` | Scroll page | `x`, `y` |
| `chrome_devtools_wait_for_selector` | Wait for element | `selector`, `timeout` |

### Inspection (6 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `chrome_devtools_take_screenshot` | Capture screenshot | `fullPage`, `pageId` |
| `chrome_devtools_take_snapshot` | Take DOM snapshot | `pageId` |
| `chrome_devtools_evaluate_script` | Execute JavaScript | `script`, `pageId` |
| `chrome_devtools_get_console_message` | Get console message | `pageId` |
| `chrome_devtools_get_network_request` | Get network request | `requestId`, `pageId` |
| `chrome_devtools_get_element_attributes` | Get element attrs | `selector`, `pageId` |

### Performance (3 tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `chrome_devtools_performance_start_trace` | Start performance trace | `categories[]`, `pageId` |
| `chrome_devtools_performance_stop_trace` | Stop trace | `pageId` |
| `chrome_devtools_performance_analyze_insight` | Analyze performance | `traceData` |

### Other Tools (3 tools)

- `chrome_devtools_resize_page` - Resize viewport
- `chrome_devtools_list_console_messages` - List console logs
- `chrome_devtools_clear_console` - Clear console

---

## 7. SHADCN UI (COMPONENTS)

**Manual name:** `shadcn_ui`
**Naming pattern:** `shadcn_ui.shadcn_ui_{tool_name}`

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `shadcn_ui_list_shadcn_components` | List all components | None |
| `shadcn_ui_get_component_details` | Get component details | `component_name` |
| `shadcn_ui_search_components` | Search components | `query` |

---

## 8. IMAGICIAN (IMAGE PROCESSING)

**Manual name:** `imagician`
**Naming pattern:** `imagician.imagician_{tool_name}`

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| `imagician_resize_image` | Resize image | `path`, `width`, `height` |
| `imagician_convert_format` | Convert image format | `path`, `format` |
| `imagician_compress_image` | Compress image | `path`, `quality` |

| `imagician_rotate_image` | Rotate image | `path`, `degrees` |
| `imagician_list_images` | List available images | None |

---

## 9. VIDEO AUDIO (30+ TOOLS)

**Manual name:** `video_audio`
**Naming pattern:** `video_audio.video_audio_{tool_name}`

### Video Tools (15+ tools)

| Tool Name | Purpose | Key Parameters |

| `video_audio_extract_video_frames` | Extract frames | `video_path`, `fps` |

## 10. TOOL DISCOVERY EXAMPLES

5: 
6: # Workflow Examples - Complex Multi-Tool Patterns
7: 
8: Five patterns demonstrating multi-tool orchestration, error handling, and state persistence in Code Mode.
9: 
10: ---
11: 
12: ## 1. OVERVIEW
13: 
14: This document provides five comprehensive workflow patterns for Code Mode UTCP.
15: 
16: **All examples use markdown code blocks** (not separate .ts files) for easier reference and embedding in documentation.
17: 
18: **Patterns covered:**
19: 
20: 1. **MyService Workflow** - Basic tool calling and data processing
21: 2. **ClickUp Workflow** - Task creation with verification
22: 3. **Notion Workflow** - Database operations with `notion_API_` prefix
23: 4. **Multi-Tool Orchestration** - Complex cross-platform workflows
24: 5. **Error Handling** - Robust patterns with fallbacks
25: 
26: ---
27: 
28: ## 2. MYSERVICE WORKFLOW
29: 
30: **Scenario:** Get all MyService sites and their CMS collections
31: 
32: **Demonstrates:**
33: - Basic tool calling
34: - Data processing within sandbox
35: - State persistence across operations
36: - Console logging
37: - Result aggregation
38: 
39: **Code:**
40: 
41: ```typescript
42: call_tool_chain({
43:   code: `
44:     console.log('Fetching MyService sites...');
45: 
46:     // Step 1: List all sites (note the naming pattern)
47:     const sitesResult = await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});
48:     const sites = sitesResult.sites;
49: 
50:     console.log(\`Found \${sites.length} site(s)\`);
51: 
52:     // Step 2: Get collections for the first site
53:     if (sites.length > 0) {
54:       const siteId = sites[0].id;
55:       const siteName = sites[0].displayName;
56: 
57:       console.log(\`Getting collections for: \${siteName}\`);
58: 
59:       const collectionsResult = await myservice.myservice_collections_list({
60:         site_id: siteId
61:       });
62: 
63:       const collections = collectionsResult.collections;
64: 
65:       return {
66:         site: {
67:           id: siteId,
68:           name: siteName,
69:           domains: sites[0].customDomains
70:         },
71:         collectionsCount: collections.length,
72:         collections: collections.map(c => ({
73:           id: c.id,
74:           name: c.displayName,
75:           slug: c.slug
76:         }))
77:       };
78:     }
79: 
80:     return { message: 'No sites found' };
81:   `
82: });
83: ```
84: 
85: **Expected Output:**

89:   result: {
90:     site: {
91:       id: "...",
92:       name: "Example Corp",

95:     collectionsCount: 21,

414: ## 6. ERROR HANDLING PATTERNS
415: 
416: **Scenario:** Robust error handling with fallback logic and partial success
417: 
418: **Demonstrates:**
419: - Try/catch error handling
420: - Graceful degradation
421: - Partial success handling
422: - Error logging
423: - Conditional execution based on success/failure
424: 
425: **Code:**
426: 
427: ```typescript
428: call_tool_chain({
429:   code: `
430:     const results = {
431:       sites: null,
432:       collections: null,
433:       errors: []
434:     };
435: 
436:     // Step 1: Attempt to fetch MyService sites with error handling
437:     try {
438:       console.log('Attempting to fetch MyService sites...');
439: 
440:       const sitesResult = await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});
441:       results.sites = sitesResult.sites;
442: 
443:       console.log(\`вң“ Successfully fetched \${results.sites.length} sites\`);
444: 
445:     } catch (error) {
446:       console.error('вң— Failed to fetch sites:', error?.message || String(error));
447:       results.errors.push({
448:         step: 'fetch_sites',
449:         error: error?.message || String(error),
450:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
451:       });
452: 
453:       // Return early if sites fetch fails
454:       return {
455:         success: false,
456:         results,
457:         message: 'Cannot proceed without sites data',
458:         failedAt: 'fetch_sites'
459:       };
460:     }
461: 
462:     // Step 2: Only proceed if we have sites
463:     if (results.sites && results.sites.length > 0) {
464:       try {
465:         const siteId = results.sites[0].id;
466:         console.log(\`Fetching collections for site: \${siteId}\`);
467: 
468:         const collectionsResult = await myservice.myservice_collections_list({
469:           site_id: siteId
470:         });
471:         results.collections = collectionsResult.collections;
472: 
473:         console.log(\`вң“ Successfully fetched \${results.collections.length} collections\`);
474: 
475:       } catch (error) {
476:         console.error('вң— Failed to fetch collections:', error?.message || String(error));
477:         results.errors.push({
478:           step: 'fetch_collections',
479:           error: error?.message || String(error),
480:           timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
481:         });
482: 
483:         // Continue execution with partial data (don't fail entire workflow)
484:         console.log('Continuing with partial data (sites only)...');
485:       }
486:     }
487: 
488:     // Step 3: Return results (success or partial success)
489:     return {
490:       success: results.errors.length === 0,
491:       partialSuccess: results.sites !== null && results.collections === null,
492:       results,
493:       errorCount: results.errors.length,
494:       timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
495:     };
496:   `,
497:   timeout: 30000
498: });
499: ```
500: 
501: **Expected Output (Full Success):**
502: 
503: ```javascript
504: {
505:   result: {
506:     success: true,
507:     partialSuccess: false,
508:     results: {
509:       sites: [{ id: "...", displayName: "Site Name", ... }],
510:       collections: [{ id: "...", displayName: "Collection", ... }],
511:       errors: []
512:     },
513:     errorCount: 0,
514:     timestamp: "2025-01-23T10:30:00Z"
515:   },
516:   logs: [
517:     "Attempting to fetch MyService sites...",
518:     "вң“ Successfully fetched 1 sites",
519:     "Fetching collections for site: ...",
520:     "вң“ Successfully fetched 21 collections"
521:   ]
522: }
523: ```
524: 
525: **Expected Output (Partial Success):**
526: 
527: ```javascript
528: {
529:   result: {
530:     success: false,
531:     partialSuccess: true,
532:     results: {
533:       sites: [{ id: "...", displayName: "Site Name", ... }],
534:       collections: null,
535:       errors: [
536:         {
537:           step: "fetch_collections",
538:           error: "Site not found or access denied",
539:           timestamp: "2025-01-23T10:30:05Z"
540:         }
541:       ]
542:     },
543:     errorCount: 1,
544:     timestamp: "2025-01-23T10:30:05Z"
545:   },
546:   logs: [
547:     "Attempting to fetch MyService sites...",
548:     "вң“ Successfully fetched 1 sites",
549:     "Fetching collections for site: ...",
550:     "вң— Failed to fetch collections: Site not found or access denied",
551:     "Continuing with partial data (sites only)..."
552:   ]
553: }
554: ```
555: 
556: **Expected Output (Complete Failure):**
557: 
558: ```javascript
559: {
560:   result: {
561:     success: false,
562:     results: {
563:       sites: null,
564:       collections: null,
565:       errors: [
566:         {
567:           step: "fetch_sites",
568:           error: "Authentication failed: Invalid API token",
569:           timestamp: "2025-01-23T10:30:00Z"
570:         }
571:       ]
572:     },
573:     message: "Cannot proceed without sites data",
574:     failedAt: "fetch_sites"
575:   },
576:   logs: [
577:     "Attempting to fetch MyService sites...",
578:     "вң— Failed to fetch sites: Authentication failed: Invalid API token"
579:   ]
580: }
581: ```
582: 
583: **Key learnings:**
584: - Try/catch prevents entire workflow from failing
585: - Partial success possible (sites fetched, collections failed)
586: - Errors logged with timestamps and step identification
587: - Early return on critical failures
588: - Graceful degradation on non-critical failures
589: - Clear success indicators (`success`, `partialSuccess`, `errorCount`)
590: - Comprehensive logging for debugging
591: 
592: **Error handling patterns:**
593: 1. **Critical failure** (sites fetch fails) вҶ’ Early return with error
594: 2. **Non-critical failure** (collections fetch fails) вҶ’ Log error, continue with partial data
595: 3. **Full success** вҶ’ Return all data
596: 4. **Partial success** вҶ’ Return what succeeded, log what failed

599: 
600: ## 7. PARALLEL EXECUTION PATTERNS
601: 
602: ### When to Parallelize
603: 
604: **вң… USE Promise.all WHEN:**
605: - Operations don't depend on each other's results
606: - Order of execution doesn't matter
607: - You need data from multiple sources before next step
608: 
609: **вң… USE Promise.allSettled WHEN:**
610: - Some operations might fail
611: - You want partial results rather than complete failure
612: - Resilience is more important than speed
613: 
614: **вқҢ DO NOT PARALLELIZE WHEN:**
615: - Operation B needs result from Operation A
616: - Order of mutations matters (create before update)
617: - API has strict rate limits
618: 
619: ### Pattern 1: Basic Parallel Fetch
620: 
621: ```typescript
622: call_tool_chain({
623:   code: `
624:     // Independent operations - execute in parallel
625:     const [sites, workspace, figmaFile] = await Promise.all([
626:       myservice.myservice_sites_list({}),
627:       clickup.clickup_get_workspace_hierarchy({}),
628:       figma.figma_get_file({ fileKey: "abc123" })
629:     ]);
630:     
631:     return {
632:       myservice: { siteCount: sites.sites.length },
633:       clickup: { spaceCount: workspace.spaces.length },
634:       figma: { fileName: figmaFile.name }
635:     };
636:   `,
637:   timeout: 45000
638: });
639: ```
640: 
641: **Performance:** ~400ms total vs ~1200ms sequential (3x faster)
642: 
643: ### Pattern 2: Graceful Failure with Promise.allSettled
644: 
645: ```typescript
646: call_tool_chain({
647:   code: `
648:     const results = await Promise.allSettled([
649:       myservice.myservice_sites_list({}),
650:       clickup.clickup_get_workspace_hierarchy({}),
651:       figma.figma_get_file({ fileKey: "invalid-key" })  // Will fail
652:     ]);
653:     
654:     const processed = results.map((result, index) => {
655:       const sources = ['myservice', 'clickup', 'figma'];
656:       if (result.status === 'fulfilled') {
657:         return { source: sources[index], success: true, data: result.value };
658:       } else {
659:         return { source: sources[index], success: false, error: result.reason.message };
660:       }
661:     });
662:     
663:     return {
664:       successCount: processed.filter(p => p.success).length,
665:       results: processed
666:     };
667:   `,
668:   timeout: 45000
669: });
670: ```
671: 
672: **Key insight:** Promise.allSettled never rejects - always returns all results with status
673: 
674: ### Pattern 3: Parallel Fetch вҶ’ Sequential Process
675: 
676: ```typescript
677: call_tool_chain({
678:   code: `
679:     // Phase 1: Fetch all data in parallel
680:     const [sites, workspace] = await Promise.all([
681:       myservice.myservice_sites_list({}),
682:       clickup.clickup_get_workspace_hierarchy({})
683:     ]);
684:     
685:     // Phase 2: Sequential processing (depends on parallel results)
686:     const firstSite = sites.sites[0];
687:     const collections = await myservice.myservice_collections_list({ 
688:       site_id: firstSite.id 
689:     });
690:     
691:     // Phase 3: Create task with combined data
692:     const task = await clickup.clickup_create_task({
693:       name: \`Sync \${firstSite.displayName} collections\`,
694:       listName: workspace.spaces[0]?.name || "Default"
695:     });
696:     
697:     return {
698:       phase1: { sites: sites.sites.length, spaces: workspace.spaces.length },
699:       phase2: { collections: collections.collections.length },
700:       phase3: { taskId: task.id }
701:     };
702:   `,
703:   timeout: 60000
704: });
705: ```
706: 
707: ### Pattern 4: Batch Operations with Chunked Parallelism
708: 
709: ```typescript
710: call_tool_chain({
711:   code: `
712:     async function processInBatches(items, batchSize, processor) {
713:       const results = [];
714:       for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i += batchSize) {
715:         const batch = items.slice(i, i + batchSize);
716:         console.log(\`Processing batch \${Math.floor(i/batchSize) + 1}\`);
717:         const batchResults = await Promise.all(batch.map(processor));
718:         results.push(...batchResults);
719:       }
720:       return results;
721:     }
722:     
723:     const collectionsResult = await myservice.myservice_collections_list({ 
724:       site_id: "your-site-id" 
725:     });
726:     
727:     // Process in batches of 3 (limit concurrent API calls)
728:     const details = await processInBatches(
729:       collectionsResult.collections.slice(0, 9),
730:       3,
731:       async (collection) => {
732:         const items = await myservice.myservice_collections_items_list_items({
733:           collection_id: collection.id,
734:           limit: 5
735:         });
736:         return { name: collection.displayName, itemCount: items.items?.length || 0 };
737:       }
738:     );
739:     
740:     return { collectionsProcessed: details.length, results: details };
741:   `,
742:   timeout: 90000
743: });
744: ```
745: 
746: **Key insight:** Batching prevents API rate limits while still parallelizing
747: 
748: ### Pattern 5: Error Handling in Parallel Context
749: 
750: ```typescript
751: call_tool_chain({
752:   code: `
753:     async function safeParallel(operations) {
754:       const results = await Promise.allSettled(
755:         operations.map(async (op) => {
756:           try {
757:             return await op.fn();
758:           } catch (error) {
759:             return { error: true, name: op.name, message: error.message };
760:           }
761:         })
762:       );
763:       
764:       return results.map((r, i) => ({
765:         name: operations[i].name,
766:         success: r.status === 'fulfilled' && !r.value?.error,
767:         data: r.status === 'fulfilled' ? r.value : null,
768:         error: r.status === 'rejected' ? r.reason.message : r.value?.message
769:       }));
770:     }
771:     
772:     return await safeParallel([
773:       { name: 'myservice', fn: () => myservice.myservice_sites_list({}) },
774:       { name: 'clickup', fn: () => clickup.clickup_get_workspace_hierarchy({}) },
775:       { name: 'figma', fn: () => figma.figma_get_file({ fileKey: "test" }) }
776:     ]);
777:   `,
778:   timeout: 60000
779: });
780: ```
781:

783: 
784: ## 8. PATTERN COMPARISON
785: 
786: ### Traditional Multi-Tool Approach (Without Code Mode)
787: 
788: **Steps required:**
789: 1. Call `myservice_sites_list` вҶ’ Wait 500ms вҶ’ Process in AI context
790: 2. Extract site ID вҶ’ Call `myservice_collections_list` вҶ’ Wait 500ms вҶ’ Process in AI context
791: 3. Call `clickup_create_task` вҶ’ Wait 500ms вҶ’ Process in AI context
792: 4. Call `myservice_cms_create_item` вҶ’ Wait 500ms вҶ’ Process in AI context
793: 
794: **Total time:** ~2000ms + 4Г— context overhead
795: **Total API calls:** 4 separate calls
796: **Context switches:** 4 times
797: **State management:** Manual across calls
798: **Error handling:** Must handle in AI layer
799: 
800: ---
801: 
802: ### Code Mode Approach
803: 
804: **Steps required:**
805: 1. Call `call_tool_chain` with entire workflow code вҶ’ Wait 300ms вҶ’ Done
806: 
807: **Total time:** ~300ms + 1Г— minimal context overhead
808: **Total API calls:** 1 code execution (internally calls 4 tools)
809: **Context switches:** 1 time
810: **State management:** Automatic within sandbox
811: **Error handling:** Built into TypeScript code
812: 
813: **Result:** **5Г— faster**, **98% less context overhead**, **simpler code**
814: 
815: ---
816: 
817: ## 9. BEST PRACTICES
818: 
819: ### 1. Always Use Console Logging
820: 
821: **Why:** Track workflow progress, debug issues, understand execution flow
822: 
823: ```typescript
824: console.log('Starting workflow...');
825: console.log(\`Processing \${data.length} items\`);
826: console.error('Error occurred:', error.message);
827: console.log('Workflow complete!');
828: ```
829: 
830: ---
831: 
832: ### 2. Structure Return Values Consistently
833: 
834: **Good:**
835: ```typescript
836: return {
837:   success: true/false,
838:   data: { /* results */ },
839:   errors: [],
840:   timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
841: };
842: ```
843: 
844: **Bad:**
845: ```typescript
846: return result;  // Unclear structure
847: ```
848: 
849: ---
850: 
851: ### 3. Use Try/Catch for Error Handling
852: 
853: **Good:**
854: ```typescript
855: try {
856:   const result = await tool.call();
857:   return { success: true, result };
858: } catch (error) {
859:   return { success: false, error: error.message };
860: }
861: ```
862: 
863: **Bad:**
864: ```typescript
865: const result = await tool.call();  // Unhandled errors crash entire workflow
866: ```
867: 
868: ---
869: 
870: ### 4. Set Appropriate Timeouts
871: 
872: **Guidelines:**
873: - Simple workflows (1-2 tools): 30s (default)
874: - Complex workflows (3-5 tools): 60s
875: - Very complex (6+ tools, large data): 120s+
876: 
877: ```typescript
878: call_tool_chain({
879:   code: `/* workflow code */`,
880:   timeout: 60000  // 60 seconds
881: });
882: ```
883: 
884: ---
885: 
886: ### 5. Use Descriptive Variable Names
887: 
888: **Good:**
889: ```typescript
890: const sitesResult = await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});
891: const firstSite = sitesResult.sites[0];
892: const collectionsForSite = await myservice.myservice_collections_list({ site_id: firstSite.id });
893: ```
894: 
895: **Bad:**
896: ```typescript
897: const r = await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});
898: const s = r.sites[0];
899: const c = await myservice.myservice_collections_list({ site_id: s.id });
900: ```
901: 
902: ---
903: 
904: ## 10. SUMMARY
905: 
906: **Five workflow patterns demonstrated:**
907: 
908: 1. **Single-tool workflow** (MyService) - Basic tool calling, data processing
909: 2. **Task creation workflow** (ClickUp) - Creation + verification pattern
910: 3. **Notion workflow** - Database querying and page creation with `notion_API_` prefix
911: 4. **Multi-tool orchestration** (Figma вҶ’ ClickUp вҶ’ MyService) - Complex data flow
912: 5. **Error handling** (MyService with fallbacks) - Robust error management
913: 6. **Parallel execution** - Promise.all, Promise.allSettled, batching patterns
914: 
915: **Key benefits of Code Mode:**
916: - State persistence across operations
917: - Single execution vs multiple round trips
918: - Built-in error handling (try/catch)
919: - Console logging captured
920: - Complex data transformations in-sandbox
921: - 5Г— faster than traditional approach
922: - 98% less context overhead
923: 
924: **When to use each pattern:**
925: - **Pattern 1:** Simple single-tool operations
926: - **Pattern 2:** Create-then-verify workflows
927: - **Pattern 3:** Notion database operations (note the `notion_API_` prefix)
928: - **Pattern 4:** Complex multi-platform workflows
929: - **Pattern 5:** Mission-critical workflows requiring error resilience
930: - **Pattern 6:** Performance-critical workflows requiring parallel execution
931: 
932: **All examples use markdown code blocks** for easier embedding in SKILL.md and documentation.
933: 
934: ---
935: 
936: ## 11. RELATED RESOURCES
937: 
938: ### Reference Files
939: - [naming_convention.md](./naming_convention.md) - Critical naming patterns used in all examples
940: - [tool_catalog.md](./tool_catalog.md) - Complete list of available tools for workflows
941: - [architecture.md](./architecture.md) - Execution environment and state persistence details
942: - [configuration.md](./configuration.md) - Configuration setup for MCP servers used in examples
943: 
944: ### Related Skills
945: - `mcp-chrome-devtools` - Browser automation workflows via bdg CLI

(End of file - total 945 lines)
</content>

6: # System Architecture - Code Mode UTCP
7: 
8: Understand how Code Mode UTCP achieves 98.7% token savings while providing access to 200+ tools.

10: ---
11: 
12: ## 1. OVERVIEW
13: 
14: ### What is Code Mode UTCP?
15: 
16: **Code Mode UTCP** is an MCP orchestration layer that executes TypeScript code with direct access to multiple MCP servers, eliminating traditional tool-calling overhead through progressive disclosure.
17: 
18: **Key innovation:** Instead of exposing 200+ tools directly to AI agents, Code Mode provides a single execution environment where tools are accessed programmatically and loaded on-demand.

86: в”Ӯ  MCP Server     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  (2 instances)     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Audio      в”Ӯ
87: в”Ӯ  (20+ tools)    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  (52 tools total)  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  (30+ tools)в”Ӯ
88: в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
89: ```
90: 
91: ---
92: 
93: ## 3. THE "2-3 MCP SERVER WALL" PROBLEM
94: 
95: ### Traditional Approach (Before Code Mode)
96: 
97: **Problem:** Each MCP tool consumes tokens in context
98: 
99: ```
100: Traditional MCP Setup:
101: в”ңв”Җ Tool 1: ~3,000 tokens (schema + description)
102: в”ңв”Җ Tool 2: ~3,000 tokens
103: в”ңв”Җ Tool 3: ~3,000 tokens
104: в”ңв”Җ ...
105: в””в”Җ Tool 47: ~3,000 tokens
106: 
107: Total: ~141,000 tokens for 47 tools
108: Practical limit: 2-3 MCP servers before context exhaustion
109: Result: UNUSABLE with 200k context window
110: ```
111: 
112: **Breakdown:**
113: - Each tool requires schema definition
114: - Descriptions for AI understanding
115: - Type definitions for parameters
116: - Example usage patterns
117: 
118: **Impact:**
119: - Can only use 2-3 MCP servers total
120: - Must choose which servers to include
121: - Context window fills quickly
122: - No room for actual conversation
123: 
124: ### Code Mode Solution
125: 
126: **Solution:** Progressive disclosure + single execution environment
127: 
128: ```
129: Code Mode UTCP Setup:
130: в”ңв”Җ call_tool_chain: ~600 tokens
131: в”ңв”Җ search_tools: ~500 tokens
132: в”ңв”Җ list_tools: ~500 tokens
133: 
134: Total: ~1,600 tokens for unlimited MCP access
135: All 8 MCP servers (200+ tools): FULLY ACCESSIBLE
136: Result: 98.7% token savings, unlimited scalability
137: ```
138: 
139: **How it works:**
140: 1. Only 3 tools visible to AI initially
141: 2. Tools discovered progressively via `search_tools()`
142: 3. Tool schemas loaded on-demand
143: 4. Execution happens in sandbox with direct tool access
144: 
145: **Impact:**
146: - Access all 200+ tools
147: - Use any combination of MCP servers
148: - Context stays clean
149: - 98.7% reduction in overhead
150: 
151: ---
152: 
153: ## 4. TOKEN ECONOMICS
154: 
155: | Approach                     | Tokens Consumed | Tools Visible | Tools Accessible | Usable?                   |
156: | ---------------------------- | --------------- | ------------- | ---------------- | ------------------------- |
157: | **Traditional (10 tools)**   | ~30,000         | 10            | 10               | вң… Yes                     |
158: | **Traditional (47 tools)**   | ~141,000        | 47            | 47               | вқҢ No (context exhaustion) |
159: | **Traditional (200+ tools)** | ~600,000+       | 200+          | 200+             | вқҢ No (impossible)         |
160: | **Code Mode UTCP**           | ~1,600          | 3             | 200+             | вң… Yes (98% savings)       |
161: 
162: **Key insight:** Code Mode breaks the linear scaling problem - adding more tools doesn't increase context overhead.
163: 
164: ---
165: 
166: ## 5. DATA FLOW
167: 
168: ### Single-Step Execution
169: 
170: **Flow:**
171: 1. AI calls `call_tool_chain({ code: "..." })`
172: 2. Code Mode MCP server receives TypeScript code
173: 3. V8 Isolate executes code with tool namespaces available
174: 4. Code calls `await manual.manual_tool(params)`
175: 5. MCP server routes call to appropriate MCP server
176: 6. Results captured and returned to V8 context
177: 7. Final results + logs returned to AI
178: 
179: **Example:**
180: 
181: ```
182: AI Agent
183:   вҶ“ (1) call_tool_chain({ code: "await myservice.myservice_sites_list({})" })
184: Code Mode MCP
185:   вҶ“ (2) Execute in V8 Isolate
186: V8 Sandbox
187:   вҶ“ (3) await myservice.myservice_sites_list({})
188: MyService MCP Server
189:   вҶ“ (4) Fetch sites from MyService API
190:   вҶ“ (5) Return { sites: [...] }
191: V8 Sandbox
192:   вҶ“ (6) Capture result
193: Code Mode MCP
194:   вҶ“ (7) Return { result: { sites: [...] }, logs: [...] }
195: AI Agent
196: ```
197: 
198: ### Multi-Step Workflow
199: 
200: **Flow:**
201: 1. AI calls `call_tool_chain` with complex workflow code
202: 2. V8 Isolate maintains state throughout execution
203: 3. Multiple async operations execute (sequential or parallel)
204: 4. Tool discovery, schema inspection, and tool calls in one execution
205: 5. Aggregated results + logs returned to AI
206: 
207: **Example (Figma вҶ’ ClickUp вҶ’ MyService):**
208: 
209: ```
210: AI Agent
211:   вҶ“ call_tool_chain({ code: "/* multi-tool workflow */" })
212: Code Mode MCP
213:   вҶ“ Execute in V8 Isolate
214: V8 Sandbox (State Persisted)
215:   в”ңв”Җ (1) const design = await figma.figma_get_file()
216:   в”Ӯ   вҶ“ Figma MCP вҶ’ Returns design data
217:   в”Ӯ   вҶ“ State: { design }
218:   в”ңв”Җ (2) const task = await clickup.clickup_create_task({ name: design.name })
219:   в”Ӯ   вҶ“ ClickUp MCP вҶ’ Returns task data
220:   в”Ӯ   вҶ“ State: { design, task }
221:   в””в”Җ (3) const cms = await myservice.myservice_collections_items_create({ data: { design, task } })
222:       вҶ“ MyService MCP вҶ’ Returns CMS item
223:       вҶ“ State: { design, task, cms }
224:       вҶ“ return { design, task, cms }
225: Code Mode MCP
226:   вҶ“ Return { result: { design, task, cms }, logs: [...] }
227: AI Agent
228: ```
229: 
230: **Key benefits:**
231: - State persists across all operations
232: - Data flows naturally between tools
233: - Single execution (no round trips)
234: - Atomic workflow (all succeed or all fail)
235: 
236: ### Progressive Tool Loading
237: 
238: **Flow:**
239: 1. AI calls `search_tools({ task_description: "..." })`
240: 2. Code Mode searches tool repository
241: 3. Returns matching tool names + descriptions (no full schemas)
242: 4. AI calls `tool_info({ tool_name: "..." })` if needed
243: 5. Full TypeScript interface returned for specific tool
244: 6. AI generates code using discovered tools
245: 7. Code executed via `call_tool_chain`
246: 
247: **Example:**
248: 
249: ```
250: AI Agent
251:   вҶ“ search_tools({ task_description: "myservice collections" })
252: Code Mode MCP
253:   вҶ“ Search tool repository (keyword matching)
254:   вҶ“ Return: [
255:       { name: "myservice.myservice_collections_list", description: "..." },
256:       { name: "myservice.myservice_collections_get", description: "..." }
257:     ]
258: AI Agent
259:   вҶ“ tool_info({ tool_name: "myservice.myservice_collections_list" })
260: Code Mode MCP
261:   вҶ“ Load full interface for specific tool
262:   вҶ“ Return: `function myservice_collections_list(params: { site_id: string }): Promise<...>`
263: AI Agent
264:   вҶ“ Generate code using interface
265:   вҶ“ call_tool_chain({ code: "await myservice.myservice_collections_list({ site_id: '...' })" })
266: Code Mode MCP
267:   вҶ“ Execute code
268: ```
269: 
270: **Why this works:**
271: - Initial search only returns tool names (minimal tokens)
272: - Full interfaces loaded only when needed
273: - Zero upfront cost for unused tools
274: - Scales to unlimited tools
275: 
276: ---
277: 
278: ## 6. EXECUTION ENVIRONMENT
279: 
280: ### V8 Isolate Sandbox
281: 
282: **What it is:** Isolated JavaScript execution context (same engine as Chrome/Node.js)
283: 
284: **Security features:**
285: - вң… Isolated execution (cannot access host file system)
286: - вң… Timeout protection (configurable, default 30s)
287: - вң… Output size limits (200,000 characters default)
288: - вң… Memory limits (prevents runaway processes)
289: - вқҢ No direct file system access (unless MCP grants it)
290: - вқҢ No network access (except via MCP tools)
291: - вқҢ No process spawning
292: 
293: **What you CAN do:**
294: - Execute TypeScript/JavaScript code
295: - Call MCP tools via namespaces
296: - Use async/await
297: - Handle errors with try/catch
298: - Persist state within single execution
299: - Use console.log() (captured and returned)
300: - Perform complex data transformations
301: - Loop, condition, function definitions
302: 
303: **What you CANNOT do:**
304: - Access host file system directly
305: - Make HTTP requests directly (use MCP tools)
306: - Spawn processes
307: - Execute indefinitely (timeout applies)
308: - Exceed output size limits
309: 
310: ### Capabilities
311: 
312: **Full TypeScript/JavaScript Support:**
313: ```typescript
314: // Variables and constants
315: const siteId = "abc123";
316: let results = [];
317: 
318: // Functions
319: function formatDate(date) {
320:   return new Date(date).toISOString();
321: }
322: 
323: // Loops
324: for (const site of sites) {
325:   results.push(site.id);
326: }
327: 
328: // Async/await
329: const data = await tool.call();
330: 
331: // Try/catch
332: try {
333:   const result = await tool.call();
334: } catch (error) {
335:   console.error('Error:', error.message);
336: }
337: 
338: // Array methods
339: const ids = items.map(i => i.id);
340: const filtered = items.filter(i => i.status === 'active');
341: ```
342: 
343: **State Persistence:**
344: ```typescript
345: // State persists throughout execution
346: const design = await figma.figma_get_file({ fileId: "..." });
347: // design variable available for rest of execution
348: 
349: const task = await clickup.clickup_create_task({
350:   name: design.name  // Can use design data
351: });
352: 
353: const cms = await myservice.myservice_collections_items_create({
354:   data: {
355:     designUrl: design.url,  // Still available
356:     taskUrl: task.url       // And task data
357:   }
358: });
359: 
360: return { design, task, cms };  // All data returned
361: ```
362: 
363: ---
364: 
365: ## 7. CONFIGURATION INTEGRATION
366: 
367: ### How .utcp_config.json Connects
368: 
369: **Configuration file defines available tools:**
370: 
371: ```json
372: {
373:   "manual_call_templates": [
374:     {
375:       "name": "myservice",          // Becomes namespace
376:       "call_template_type": "mcp",
377:       "config": {
378:         "mcpServers": {
379:           "myservice": {
380:             "transport": "stdio",
381:             "command": "npx",
382:             "args": ["mcp-remote", "https://mcp.myservice.com/sse"]
383:           }
384:         }
385:       }
386:     }
387:   ]
388: }
389: ```
390: 
391: **Runtime behavior:**
392: 1. Code Mode loads `.utcp_config.json` on startup
393: 2. Creates namespace for each `name` field (`myservice`, `clickup`, etc.)
394: 3. Registers MCP servers with tool repository
395: 4. Makes namespaces available in V8 sandbox
396: 5. Routes tool calls to appropriate MCP server
397: 
398: **In execution:**
399: ```typescript
400: // "myservice" namespace available because config has:
401: // { "name": "myservice", ... }
402: 
403: await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});
404: //    вҶ‘ namespace from config
405: ```
406: 
407: ---
408: 
409: ## 8. PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS
410: 
411: ### Execution Speed
412: 
413: | Operation                 | Time   | Notes                             |
414: | ------------------------- | ------ | --------------------------------- |
415: | **Single tool call**      | ~300ms | Including V8 startup + execution  |
416: | **Multi-tool (3 tools)**  | ~500ms | Sequential execution              |
417: | **Multi-tool (5 tools)**  | ~800ms | Sequential execution              |
418: | **Multi-tool (parallel)** | ~400ms | Promise.all() for independent ops |
419: 
420: **Comparison to traditional:**
421: - Traditional (3 tools sequentially): ~1500ms (3Г— 500ms)
422: - Code Mode (3 tools sequentially): ~500ms
423: - **Result:** 3Г— faster
424: 
425: ### Token Consumption
426: 
427: | Approach        | Initial Load | Per Tool         | Total (47 tools) | Total (200+ tools) |
428: | --------------- | ------------ | ---------------- | ---------------- | ------------------ |
429: | **Traditional** | ~5k          | ~3k              | ~141k            | ~600k+             |
430: | **Code Mode**   | ~1.6k        | ~0 (progressive) | ~1.6k            | ~1.6k              |
431: 
432: **Savings:** 98.7% reduction in context overhead
433: 
434: ### Scalability
435: 
436: **Traditional approach:** Linear scaling (unusable beyond 2-3 servers)
437: 
438: ```
439: Tools:     10      20      30      47      100     200
440: Tokens:    30k     60k     90k    141k    300k    600k
441: Usable?    вң…      вң…      вқҢ     вқҢ      вқҢ      вқҢ
442: ```
443: 
444: **Code Mode approach:** Constant overhead (unlimited servers)
445: 
446: ```
447: Tools:     10      20      30      47      100     200+
448: Tokens:    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k
449: Usable?    вң…      вң…      вң…     вң…      вң…      вң…
450: ```
451: 
452: ---
453: 
454: ## 9. ARCHITECTURE BENEFITS
455: 
456: ### 1. Context Efficiency
457: 
458: **Before:** 141,000 tokens for 47 tools
459: **After:** 1,600 tokens for 200+ tools
460: **Savings:** 98.7%
461: 
462: ### 2. Execution Speed
463: 
464: **Before:** 15+ API round trips for multi-tool workflow
465: **After:** 1 execution (internally calls multiple tools)
466: **Improvement:** 5Г— faster
467: 
468: ### 3. State Management
469: 
470: **Before:** Manual state tracking across AI context
471: **After:** Automatic state persistence in V8 sandbox
472: **Benefit:** Natural data flow between operations
473: 
474: ### 4. Error Handling
475: 
476: **Before:** Error handling in AI layer (complex)
477: **After:** Built-in try/catch in TypeScript
478: **Benefit:** Robust, familiar error patterns
479: 
480: ### 5. Developer Experience
481: 
482: **Before:** Multiple tool calls, context switching, manual orchestration
483: **After:** Single TypeScript workflow, natural programming
484: **Benefit:** Faster development, fewer bugs
485: 
486: ---
487: 
488: ## 10. SUMMARY
489: 
490: **Architecture highlights:**
491: 
492: 1. **Three-layer system:** AI Agent вҶ’ Code Mode MCP вҶ’ MCP Servers
493: 2. **Progressive disclosure:** Tools loaded on-demand (zero upfront cost)
494: 3. **V8 Isolate sandbox:** Secure, isolated execution environment
495: 4. **Token efficiency:** 98.7% reduction vs traditional approach
496: 5. **Execution speed:** 5Г— faster than traditional multi-tool workflows
497: 6. **Unlimited scalability:** Add MCP servers without context penalty
498: 
499: **Key innovation:** Breaking the linear scaling problem through progressive tool disclosure and sandboxed execution.
500: 
501: **Result:** 200+ tools accessible with same context overhead as 3 tools in traditional approach.
502: 
503: ---
504: 
505: ## 11. RELATED RESOURCES
506: 
507: ### Reference Files
508: - [configuration.md](./configuration.md) - Complete configuration guide for .utcp_config.json
509: - [naming_convention.md](./naming_convention.md) - Critical naming patterns for tool calls
510: - [tool_catalog.md](./tool_catalog.md) - Complete list of 200+ available MCP tools
511: - [workflows.md](./workflows.md) - Complex multi-tool workflow examples
512: 
513: ### Related Skills
514: (no Code Mode-related skills)

(End of file - total 514 lines)
</content>

# System Architecture - Code Mode UTCP

Understand how Code Mode UTCP achieves 98.7% token savings while providing access to 200+ tools.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### What is Code Mode UTCP?

**Code Mode UTCP** is an MCP orchestration layer that executes TypeScript code with direct access to multiple MCP servers, eliminating traditional tool-calling overhead through progressive disclosure.

**Key innovation:** Instead of exposing 200+ tools directly to AI agents, Code Mode provides a single execution environment where tools are accessed programmatically and loaded on-demand.

# System Architecture - Code Mode UTCP

Understand how Code Mode UTCP achieves 98.7% token savings while providing access to 200+ tools.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### What is Code Mode UTCP?

**Code Mode UTCP** is an MCP orchestration layer that executes TypeScript code with direct access to multiple MCP servers, eliminating traditional tool-calling overhead through progressive disclosure.

**Key innovation:** Instead of exposing 200+ tools directly to AI agents, Code Mode provides a single execution environment where tools are accessed programmatically and loaded on-demand.

---

## 2. ARCHITECTURE DIAGRAM

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  Layer 1: AI Agent (OpenCode, Cline, etc.)                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Sees: Only 3 tools in context (~1.6k tokens)               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў call_tool_chain     (Execute TypeScript)            в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў search_tools        (Progressive discovery)         в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў list_tools          (List all available)            в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                           в”Ӯ
                           в”Ӯ Sends TypeScript Code
                           в”Ӯ
                           в–ј
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  Layer 2: Code Mode MCP Server                              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  TypeScript Execution Environment (V8 Isolate)        в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Security Sandbox                               в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вҖў Isolated execution context                   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вҖў No file system access (unless MCP grants)     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ

---

## 3. THE "2-3 MCP SERVER WALL" PROBLEM

### Traditional Approach (Before Code Mode)

**Problem:** Each MCP tool consumes tokens in context

```
Traditional MCP Setup:
в”ңв”Җ Tool 1: ~3,000 tokens (schema + description)
в”ңв”Җ Tool 2: ~3,000 tokens
в”ңв”Җ Tool 3: ~3,000 tokens
в”ңв”Җ ...
в””в”Җ Tool 47: ~3,000 tokens

Total: ~141,000 tokens for 47 tools
Practical limit: 2-3 MCP servers before context exhaustion
Result: UNUSABLE with 200k context window
```

**Breakdown:**
- Each tool requires schema definition
- Descriptions for AI understanding
- Type definitions for parameters
- Example usage patterns

**Impact:**
- Can only use 2-3 MCP servers total
- Must choose which servers to include
- Context window fills quickly
- No room for actual conversation

### Code Mode Solution

**Solution:** Progressive disclosure + single execution environment

```
Code Mode UTCP Setup:
в”ңв”Җ call_tool_chain: ~600 tokens
в”ңв”Җ search_tools: ~500 tokens
в”ңв”Җ list_tools: ~500 tokens

Total: ~1,600 tokens for unlimited MCP access
All 8 MCP servers (200+ tools): FULLY ACCESSIBLE
Result: 98.7% token savings, unlimited scalability
```

**How it works:**
1. Only 3 tools visible to AI initially
2. Tools discovered progressively via `search_tools()`
3. Tool schemas loaded on-demand
4. Execution happens in sandbox with direct tool access

**Impact:**
- Access all 200+ tools
- Use any combination of MCP servers
- Context stays clean
- 98.7% reduction in overhead

---

## 4. TOKEN ECONOMICS

| Approach                     | Tokens Consumed | Tools Visible | Tools Accessible | Usable?                   |
| ---------------------------- | --------------- | ------------- | ---------------- | ------------------------- |
| **Traditional (10 tools)**   | ~30,000         | 10            | 10               | вң… Yes                     |
| **Traditional (47 tools)**   | ~141,000        | 47            | 47               | вқҢ No (context exhaustion) |
| **Traditional (200+ tools)** | ~600,000+       | 200+          | 200+             | вқҢ No (impossible)         |
| **Code Mode UTCP**           | ~1,600          | 3             | 200+             | вң… Yes (98% savings)       |

**Key insight:** Code Mode breaks the linear scaling problem - adding more tools doesn't increase context overhead.

---

## 5. DATA FLOW

### Single-Step Execution

**Flow:**
1. AI calls `call_tool_chain({ code: "..." })`
2. Code Mode MCP server receives TypeScript code
3. V8 Isolate executes code with tool namespaces available
4. Code calls `await manual.manual_tool(params)`
5. MCP server routes call to appropriate MCP server
6. Results captured and returned to V8 context
7. Final results + logs returned to AI

**Example:**

```
AI Agent
  вҶ“ (1) call_tool_chain({ code: "await myservice.myservice_sites_list({})" })
Code Mode MCP
  вҶ“ (2) Execute in V8 Isolate
V8 Sandbox
  вҶ“ (3) await myservice.myservice_sites_list({})
MyService MCP Server
  вҶ“ (4) Fetch sites from MyService API
  вҶ“ (5) Return { sites: [...] }
V8 Sandbox
  вҶ“ (6) Capture result
Code Mode MCP
  вҶ“ (7) Return { result: { sites: [...] }, logs: [...] }
AI Agent
```

### Multi-Step Workflow

**Flow:**
1. AI calls `call_tool_chain` with complex workflow code
2. V8 Isolate maintains state throughout execution
3. Multiple async operations execute (sequential or parallel)
4. Tool discovery, schema inspection, and tool calls in one execution
5. Aggregated results + logs returned to AI

**Example (Figma вҶ’ ClickUp вҶ’ MyService):**

```
AI Agent
  вҶ“ call_tool_chain({ code: "/* multi-tool workflow */" })
Code Mode MCP
  вҶ“ Execute in V8 Isolate
V8 Sandbox (State Persisted)
  в”ңв”Җ (1) const design = await figma.figma_get_file()
  в”Ӯ   вҶ“ Figma MCP вҶ’ Returns design data
  в”Ӯ   вҶ“ State: { design }
  в”ңв”Җ (2) const task = await clickup.clickup_create_task({ name: design.name })
  в”Ӯ   вҶ“ ClickUp MCP вҶ’ Returns task data
  в”Ӯ   вҶ“ State: { design, task }
  в””в”Җ (3) const cms = await myservice.myservice_collections_items_create({ data: { design, task } })
      вҶ“ MyService MCP вҶ’ Returns CMS item
      вҶ“ State: { design, task, cms }
      вҶ“ return { design, task, cms }
Code Mode MCP
  вҶ“ Return { result: { design, task, cms }, logs: [...] }
AI Agent
```

**Key benefits:**
- State persists across all operations
- Data flows naturally between tools
- Single execution (no round trips)
- Atomic workflow (all succeed or all fail)

### Progressive Tool Loading

**Flow:**
1. AI calls `search_tools({ task_description: "..." })`
2. Code Mode searches tool repository
3. Returns matching tool names + descriptions (no full schemas)
4. AI calls `tool_info({ tool_name: "..." })` if needed
5. Full TypeScript interface returned for specific tool
6. AI generates code using discovered tools
7. Code executed via `call_tool_chain`

**Example:**

```
AI Agent
  вҶ“ search_tools({ task_description: "myservice collections" })
Code Mode MCP
  вҶ“ Search tool repository (keyword matching)
  вҶ“ Return: [
      { name: "myservice.myservice_collections_list", description: "..." },
      { name: "myservice.myservice_collections_get", description: "..." }
    ]
AI Agent
  вҶ“ tool_info({ tool_name: "myservice.myservice_collections_list" })
Code Mode MCP
  вҶ“ Load full interface for specific tool
  вҶ“ Return: `function myservice_collections_list(params: { site_id: string }): Promise<...>`
AI Agent
  вҶ“ Generate code using interface
  вҶ“ call_tool_chain({ code: "await myservice.myservice_collections_list({ site_id: '...' })" })
Code Mode MCP
  вҶ“ Execute code
```

**Why this works:**
- Initial search only returns tool names (minimal tokens)
- Full interfaces loaded only when needed
- Zero upfront cost for unused tools
- Scales to unlimited tools

---

## 6. EXECUTION ENVIRONMENT

### V8 Isolate Sandbox

**What it is:** Isolated JavaScript execution context (same engine as Chrome/Node.js)

**Security features:**
- вң… Isolated execution (cannot access host file system)
- вң… Timeout protection (configurable, default 30s)
- вң… Output size limits (200,000 characters default)
- вң… Memory limits (prevents runaway processes)
- вқҢ No direct file system access (unless MCP grants it)
- вқҢ No network access (except via MCP tools)
- вқҢ No process spawning

**What you CAN do:**
- Execute TypeScript/JavaScript code
- Call MCP tools via namespaces
- Use async/await
- Handle errors with try/catch
- Persist state within single execution
- Use console.log() (captured and returned)
- Perform complex data transformations
- Loop, condition, function definitions

**What you CANNOT do:**
- Access host file system directly
- Make HTTP requests directly (use MCP tools)
- Spawn processes
- Execute indefinitely (timeout applies)
- Exceed output size limits

### Capabilities

**Full TypeScript/JavaScript Support:**
```typescript
// Variables and constants
const siteId = "abc123";
let results = [];

// Functions
function formatDate(date) {
  return new Date(date).toISOString();
}

// Loops
for (const site of sites) {
  results.push(site.id);
}

// Async/await
const data = await tool.call();

// Try/catch
try {
  const result = await tool.call();
} catch (error) {
  console.error('Error:', error.message);
}

// Array methods
const ids = items.map(i => i.id);
const filtered = items.filter(i => i.status === 'active');
```

**State Persistence:**
```typescript
// State persists throughout execution
const design = await figma.figma_get_file({ fileId: "..." });
// design variable available for rest of execution

const task = await clickup.clickup_create_task({
  name: design.name  // Can use design data
});

const cms = await myservice.myservice_collections_items_create({
  data: {
    designUrl: design.url,  // Still available
    taskUrl: task.url       // And task data
  }
});

return { design, task, cms };  // All data returned
```

---

## 7. CONFIGURATION INTEGRATION

### How .utcp_config.json Connects

**Configuration file defines available tools:**

```json
{
  "manual_call_templates": [
    {
      "name": "myservice",          // Becomes namespace
      "call_template_type": "mcp",
      "config": {
        "mcpServers": {
          "myservice": {
            "transport": "stdio",
            "command": "npx",
            "args": ["mcp-remote", "https://mcp.myservice.com/sse"]
          }
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

**Runtime behavior:**
1. Code Mode loads `.utcp_config.json` on startup
2. Creates namespace for each `name` field (`myservice`, `clickup`, etc.)
3. Registers MCP servers with tool repository
4. Makes namespaces available in V8 sandbox
5. Routes tool calls to appropriate MCP server

**In execution:**
```typescript
// "myservice" namespace available because config has:
// { "name": "myservice", ... }

await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});
//    вҶ‘ namespace from config
```

---

## 8. PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS

### Execution Speed

| Operation                 | Time   | Notes                             |
| ------------------------- | ------ | --------------------------------- |
| **Single tool call**      | ~300ms | Including V8 startup + execution  |
| **Multi-tool (3 tools)**  | ~500ms | Sequential execution              |
| **Multi-tool (5 tools)**  | ~800ms | Sequential execution              |
| **Multi-tool (parallel)** | ~400ms | Promise.all() for independent ops |

**Comparison to traditional:**
- Traditional (3 tools sequentially): ~1500ms (3Г— 500ms)
- Code Mode (3 tools sequentially): ~500ms
- **Result:** 3Г— faster

### Token Consumption

| Approach        | Initial Load | Per Tool         | Total (47 tools) | Total (200+ tools) |
| --------------- | ------------ | ---------------- | ---------------- | ------------------ |
| **Traditional** | ~5k          | ~3k              | ~141k            | ~600k+             |
| **Code Mode**   | ~1.6k        | ~0 (progressive) | ~1.6k            | ~1.6k              |

**Savings:** 98.7% reduction in context overhead

### Scalability

**Traditional approach:** Linear scaling (unusable beyond 2-3 servers)

```
Tools:     10      20      30      47      100     200
Tokens:    30k     60k     90k    141k    300k    600k
Usable?    вң…      вң…      вқҢ     вқҢ      вқҢ      вқҢ
```

**Code Mode approach:** Constant overhead (unlimited servers)

```
Tools:     10      20      30      47      100     200+
Tokens:    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k
Usable?    вң…      вң…      вң…     вң…      вң…      вң…
```

---

## 9. ARCHITECTURE BENEFITS

### 1. Context Efficiency

**Before:** 141,000 tokens for 47 tools
**After:** 1,600 tokens for 200+ tools
**Savings:** 98.7%

### 2. Execution Speed

**Before:** 15+ API round trips for multi-tool workflow
**After:** 1 execution (internally calls multiple tools)
**Improvement:** 5Г— faster

### 3. State Management

**Before:** Manual state tracking across AI context
**After:** Automatic state persistence in V8 sandbox
**Benefit:** Natural data flow between operations

### 4. Error Handling

**Before:** Error handling in AI layer (complex)
**After:** Built-in try/catch in TypeScript
**Benefit:** Robust, familiar error patterns

### 5. Developer Experience

**Before:** Multiple tool calls, context switching, manual orchestration
**After:** Single TypeScript workflow, natural programming
**Benefit:** Faster development, fewer bugs

---

## 10. SUMMARY

**Architecture highlights:**

1. **Three-layer system:** AI Agent вҶ’ Code Mode MCP вҶ’ MCP Servers
2. **Progressive disclosure:** Tools loaded on-demand (zero upfront cost)
3. **V8 Isolate sandbox:** Secure, isolated execution environment
4. **Token efficiency:** 98.7% reduction vs traditional approach
5. **Execution speed:** 5Г— faster than traditional multi-tool workflows
6. **Unlimited scalability:** Add MCP servers without context penalty

**Key innovation:** Breaking the linear scaling problem through progressive tool disclosure and sandboxed execution.

**Result:** 200+ tools accessible with same context overhead as 3 tools in traditional approach.

---

20: Copy this template to `.utcp_config.json` in your project root, then customize the `manual_call_templates` array with your desired MCP servers. Reference credentials using `${VARIABLE_NAME}` syntax.
21: 
22: ---
23: 
24: ## 2. TEMPLATE
25: 
26: ```json
27: {
28:   "load_variables_from": [
29:     {
30:       "variable_loader_type": "dotenv",
31:       "env_file_path": ".env"
32:     }
33:   ],
34:   "tool_repository": {
35:     "tool_repository_type": "in_memory"
36:   },
37:   "tool_search_strategy": {
38:     "tool_search_strategy_type": "tag_and_description_word_match"
39:   },
40:   "manual_call_templates": [
41:     {
42:       "name": "myservice",
43:       "call_template_type": "mcp",
44:       "config": {
45:         "mcpServers": {
46:           "myservice": {
47:             "transport": "stdio",
48:             "command": "npx",
49:             "args": ["mcp-remote", "https://mcp.myservice.com/sse"],
50:             "env": {}
51:           }
52:         }
53:       }
54:     },
55:     {
56:       "name": "clickup",
57:       "call_template_type": "mcp",
58:       "config": {
59:         "mcpServers": {
60:           "clickup": {
61:             "transport": "stdio",
62:             "command": "npx",
63:             "args": ["-y", "@taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server"],
64:             "env": {
65:               "CLICKUP_API_KEY": "${CLICKUP_API_KEY}",
66:               "CLICKUP_TEAM_ID": "${CLICKUP_TEAM_ID}"
67:             }
68:           }
69:         }
70:       }
71:     },
72:     {
73:       "name": "figma",
74:       "call_template_type": "mcp",
75:       "config": {
76:         "mcpServers": {
77:           "figma": {
78:             "transport": "stdio",
79:             "command": "npx",
80:             "args": ["-y", "figma-developer-mcp", "--stdio"],
81:             "env": {
82:               "FIGMA_API_KEY": "${FIGMA_API_KEY}"
83:             }
84:           }
85:         }
86:       }
87:     },
88:     {
89:       "name": "notion",
90:       "call_template_type": "mcp",
91:       "config": {
92:         "mcpServers": {
93:           "notion": {
94:             "transport": "stdio",
95:             "command": "npx",
96:             "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-notion"],
97:             "env": {
98:               "NOTION_TOKEN": "${NOTION_TOKEN}"
99:             }
100:           }
101:         }
102:       }
103:     },
104:     {
105:       "name": "chrome_devtools_1",
106:       "call_template_type": "mcp",
107:       "config": {
108:         "mcpServers": {
109:           "chrome-devtools-1": {
110:             "transport": "stdio",
111:             "command": "npx",
112:             "args": ["chrome-devtools-mcp@latest"],
113:             "env": {}
114:           }
115:         }
116:       }
117:     },
118:     {
119:       "name": "chrome_devtools_2",
120:       "call_template_type": "mcp",
121:       "config": {
122:         "mcpServers": {
123:           "chrome-devtools-2": {
124:             "transport": "stdio",
125:             "command": "npx",
126:             "args": ["chrome-devtools-mcp@latest"],
127:             "env": {}
128:           }
129:         }
130:       }
131:     }
132:   ]
133: }
134: ```
135: 
136: ---
137: 
138: ## 3. CONFIGURATION SECTIONS
139: 
140: ### load_variables_from
141: 
142: **Purpose**: Load environment variables from external sources for secure credential management.
143: 
144: **Configuration:**
145: ```json
146: "load_variables_from": [
147:   {
148:     "variable_loader_type": "dotenv",
149:     "env_file_path": ".env"
150:   }
151: ]
152: ```
153: 
154: **Options:**
155: - `variable_loader_type`: `"dotenv"` (loads from .env file)
156: - `env_file_path`: Path to .env file (default: `".env"`)
157: 
158: **Best Practice**: Store all API keys and tokens in `.env` file, never commit to version control.
159: 
160: ### tool_repository
161: 
162: **Purpose**: Configure how tools are stored and accessed.
163: 
164: **Configuration:**
165: ```json
166: "tool_repository": {
167:   "tool_repository_type": "in_memory"
168: }
169: ```
170: 
171: **Options:**
172: - `tool_repository_type`: `"in_memory"` (stores tools in memory for fast access)
173: 
174: **Note**: In-memory storage provides the best performance for Code Mode operations.
175: 
176: ### tool_search_strategy
177: 
178: **Purpose**: Define how tool discovery works when using `search_tools()`.
179: 
180: **Configuration:**
181: ```json
182: "tool_search_strategy": {
183:   "tool_search_strategy_type": "tag_and_description_word_match"
184: }
185: ```
186: 
187: **Options:**
188: - `tool_search_strategy_type`: `"tag_and_description_word_match"` (searches tool names and descriptions)
189: 
190: **Usage**: Enables semantic tool discovery via `search_tools({ task_description: "..." })`.
191: 
192: ### manual_call_templates
193: 
194: **Purpose**: Define all MCP servers that Code Mode can access.
195: 
196: **Structure**: Array of manual configurations, each with:
197: - `name`: Manual name (used as namespace in code: `manual.manual_tool()`)
198: - `call_template_type`: `"mcp"` (MCP server type)
199: - `config.mcpServers`: Object with MCP server configuration
200: 
201: **Critical Naming Pattern**: Tools are called as `{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}`
202: - Example: `myservice.myservice_sites_list()`
203: - See [naming_convention.md](../references/naming_convention.md) for complete guide
204: 
205: ---
206: 
207: ## 4. MCP SERVER CONFIGURATIONS
208: 
209: ### MyService (Remote MCP)
210: 
211: ```json
212: {
213:   "name": "myservice",
214:   "call_template_type": "mcp",
215:   "config": {
216:     "mcpServers": {
217:       "myservice": {
218:         "transport": "stdio",
219:         "command": "npx",
220:         "args": ["mcp-remote", "https://mcp.myservice.com/sse"],
221:         "env": {}
222:       }
223:     }
224:   }
225: }
226: ```
227: 
228: **Features**: 40+ tools for sites, collections, pages, CMS items
229: **Authentication**: Handled by MyService's remote MCP server via browser OAuth flow. When first connecting, you'll be prompted to authenticate via MyService's web interface. Credentials are managed server-side - no local token storage required.
230: **Transport**: `stdio` (standard input/output)
231: 
232: ### ClickUp
233: 
234: ```json
235: {
236:   "name": "clickup",
237:   "call_template_type": "mcp",
238:   "config": {
239:     "mcpServers": {
240:       "clickup": {
241:         "transport": "stdio",
242:         "command": "npx",
243:         "args": ["-y", "@taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server"],
244:         "env": {
245:           "CLICKUP_API_KEY": "${CLICKUP_API_KEY}",
246:           "CLICKUP_TEAM_ID": "${CLICKUP_TEAM_ID}"
247:         }
248:       }
249:     }
250:   }
251: }
252: ```
253: 
254: **Features**: 20+ tools for tasks, lists, workspaces
255: **Authentication**: Requires `clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY` and `clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID` in .env (prefixed with the manual name)
256: **Package**: `@taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server` (installed on-demand via npx)
257: 
258: ### Figma
259: 
260: ```json
261: {
262:   "name": "figma",
263:   "call_template_type": "mcp",
264:   "config": {
265:     "mcpServers": {
266:       "figma": {
267:         "transport": "stdio",
268:         "command": "npx",
269:         "args": ["-y", "figma-developer-mcp", "--stdio"],
270:         "env": {
271:           "FIGMA_API_KEY": "${FIGMA_API_KEY}"
272:         }
273:       }
274:     }
275:   }
276: }
277: ```
278: 
279: **Features**: 18 tools for files, comments, images, components, styles
280: **Authentication**: Requires `figma_FIGMA_API_KEY` in .env (prefixed with the manual name)
281: **Package**: `figma-developer-mcp` (Framelink third-party)
282: 
283: ### Notion
284: 
285: ```json
286: {
287:   "name": "notion",
288:   "call_template_type": "mcp",
289:   "config": {
290:     "mcpServers": {
291:       "notion": {
292:         "transport": "stdio",
293:         "command": "npx",
294:         "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-notion"],
295:         "env": {
296:           "NOTION_TOKEN": "${NOTION_TOKEN}"
297:         }
298:       }
299:     }
300:   }
301: }
302: ```
303: 
304: **Features**: 20+ tools for pages, databases, blocks
305: **Authentication**: Requires `notion_NOTION_TOKEN` in .env (prefixed with the manual name)
306: **Package**: `@modelcontextprotocol/server-notion` (official MCP server)
307: 
308: ### Chrome DevTools (Multiple Instances)
309: 
310: ```json
311: {
312:   "name": "chrome_devtools_1",
313:   "call_template_type": "mcp",
314:   "config": {
315:     "mcpServers": {
316:       "chrome-devtools-1": {
317:         "transport": "stdio",
318:         "command": "npx",
319:         "args": ["chrome-devtools-mcp@latest"],
320:         "env": {}
321:       }
322:     }
323:   }
324: }
325: ```
326: 
327: **Features**: 26 tools per instance (navigation, interaction, inspection)
328: **Authentication**: No credentials needed
329: **Multiple Instances**: Use unique names (`chrome_devtools_1`, `chrome_devtools_2`) for parallel browser sessions
330: **Package**: `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest` (always use latest version)
331: 
332: ---
333: 
334: ## 5. ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES
335: 
336: **Required .env file**: Create `.env` in project root with:
337: 
338: > **вҡ пёҸ CRITICAL**: Code Mode requires **prefixed** variable names in your `.env` file. The prefix is the `name` field from your manual configuration below.
339: 
340: ```bash
341: # ClickUp (prefixed with manual name "clickup")
342: clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your_api_key_here
343: clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=your_team_id_here
344: 
345: # Figma (prefixed with manual name "figma")
346: figma_FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your_token_here
347: 
348: # Notion (prefixed with manual name "notion")
349: notion_NOTION_TOKEN=ntn_your_token_here
350: ```
351: 
352: **Why Prefixed?** Code Mode automatically prepends `{manual_name}_` to all environment variable references. If your config has `"name": "clickup"` and references `${CLICKUP_API_KEY}`, Code Mode looks for `clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY` in your `.env` file.
353: 
354: **Security:**
355: - Add `.env` to `.gitignore` (never commit credentials)
356: - Use `${VARIABLE_NAME}` syntax in config to reference env vars
357: - See [env_template.md](./env_template.md) for complete template
358: 
359: ---
360: 
361: ## 6. CUSTOMIZATION GUIDE
362: 
363: ### Adding a New MCP Server
364: 
365: **Steps:**
366: 1. Find the MCP server package (search npm or MCP directory)
367: 2. Add new object to `manual_call_templates` array
368: 3. Configure `name`, `command`, `args`, and `env` variables
369: 4. Add required credentials to `.env` file
370: 5. Test with `search_tools()` to verify tools are available
371: 
372: **Example: Adding GitHub MCP**
373: 
374: ```json
375: {
376:   "name": "github",
377:   "call_template_type": "mcp",
378:   "config": {
379:     "mcpServers": {
380:       "github": {
381:         "transport": "stdio",
382:         "command": "npx",
383:         "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"],
384:         "env": {
385:           "GITHUB_TOKEN": "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
386:         }
387:       }
388:     }
389:   }
390: }
391: ```
392: 
393: Then add to `.env`:
394: ```bash
395: GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_your_token_here
396: ```
397: 
398: ### Removing an MCP Server
399: 
400: **Steps:**
401: 1. Remove the manual object from `manual_call_templates` array
402: 2. Remove associated env vars from `.env` (optional)
403: 3. Tools will no longer be available via Code Mode
404: 
405: ### Changing Manual Names
406: 
407: **Important**: Manual name determines namespace for tool calls.
408: 
409: **Example:**
410: - Config: `"name": "myservice_prod"`
411: - Tool calls: `myservice_prod.myservice_prod_sites_list()`
412: 
413: **Use case**: Multiple instances of same MCP server with different credentials
414: ```json
415: {"name": "myservice_prod", ...},
416: {"name": "myservice_staging", ...}
417: ```
418: 
419: ---
420: 
421: ## 7. VALIDATION
422: 
423: **Validate configuration:**
424: 
425: 1. **File syntax**: Ensure valid JSON (use `cat .utcp_config.json | jq` to check)
426: 2. **Environment variables**: Verify all referenced `${VAR_NAME}` exist in `.env`
427: 3. **Tool discovery**: Test with `list_tools()` after setup
428: 
429: **Common mistakes:**
430: - Missing comma between manual objects
431: - Incorrect env var syntax (use `${VAR}` not `$VAR`)
432: - Manual name mismatch between config and code
433: - Missing npx `-y` flag (causes interactive prompt)
434: 
435: ---
436: 
437: ## 8. RELATED RESOURCES
438: 
439: ### Templates
440: - [env_template.md](./env_template.md) - Complete .env file template with credential sources
441: 
442: ### Reference Files
443: - [naming_convention.md](../references/naming_convention.md) - Tool naming conventions and invocation patterns (CRITICAL for tool usage)
444: - [configuration.md](../references/configuration.md) - Comprehensive configuration guide for Code Mode UTCP
445: - [tool_catalog.md](../references/tool_catalog.md) - Complete catalog of available MCP tools by server
446: 
447: **Template Version**: 1.0.0
448: **Last Updated**: 2025-01-23
449: **Compatibility**: Code Mode UTCP v1.x

(End of file - total 449 lines)
</content>

# Configuration Template

Copy-ready `.utcp_config.json` template for Code Mode UTCP with pre-configured MCP servers.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Base configuration file for Code Mode UTCP with progressive tool loading and environment variable support. Includes pre-configured templates for MyService, ClickUp, Figma, Notion, and Chrome DevTools.

### Usage

Copy this template to `.utcp_config.json` in your project root, then customize the `manual_call_templates` array with your desired MCP servers. Reference credentials using `${VARIABLE_NAME}` syntax.
