<content>
1: ---
2: title: Environment Variables Template
3: description: Complete .env file template with placeholders for all MCP server credentials and API tokens.
4: ---
5: 
6: # Environment Variables Template
7: 
8: Copy-ready `.env` template for Code Mode UTCP authentication with all supported MCP servers.
9: 
10: ---
11: 
12: ## 1. OVERVIEW
13: 
14: ### Purpose
15: 
16: Centralized environment variables for Code Mode UTCP authentication and configuration. Use this template to securely manage credentials for ClickUp, Figma, Notion, GitHub, MyService, and Chrome DevTools MCP servers.
17: 
18: ### Usage
19: 
20: Copy this template to `.env` in your project root, then replace placeholder values with your actual credentials. Never commit `.env` to version control - add it to `.gitignore` immediately.
21: 
22: ---
23: 
24: ## 2. TEMPLATE
25: 
26: ```bash
27: # Code Mode UTCP - Environment Variables Template
28: # Copy this to .env and fill in your actual credentials
29: #
30: # вҡ пёҸ CRITICAL: Code Mode uses PREFIXED variable names!
31: # Format: {manual_name}_{VAR} where manual_name is from .utcp_config.json
32: # Example: If your config has "name": "clickup", use clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY
33: 
34: # ClickUp Configuration
35: # (Code Mode prefixes all env vars with the manual name from .utcp_config.json)
36: clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your_api_key_here
37: clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=your_team_id_here
38: 
39: # Figma Configuration
40: # (Code Mode prefixes all env vars with the manual name from .utcp_config.json)
41: figma_FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your_token_here
42: 
43: # Notion Configuration
44: # (Code Mode prefixes all env vars with the manual name from .utcp_config.json)
45: notion_NOTION_TOKEN=ntn_your_token_here
46: 
47: # GitHub Configuration (Optional - add GitHub MCP to .utcp_config.json if needed)
48: # See config_template.md Section 5 for GitHub MCP setup
49: # (Code Mode prefixes all env vars with the manual name from .utcp_config.json)
50: github_GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_your_token_here
51: 
52: # MyService Configuration (if using direct API, not remote MCP)
53: # (Code Mode prefixes all env vars with the manual name from .utcp_config.json)
54: myservice_MYSERVICE_API_TOKEN=your_myservice_token_here
55: 
56: # Chrome DevTools (usually no auth needed)
57: # No environment variables required
58: 
59: # Add additional MCP server credentials below as needed
60: # Remember: Always prefix with the manual name from your .utcp_config.json!
61: ```
62: 
63: ---
64: 
65: ## 3. CREDENTIAL SOURCES
66: 
67: ### ClickUp
68: 
69: **Required Variables:**
70: - `clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY` - Personal API key for ClickUp workspace (prefixed)
71: - `clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID` - ClickUp team/workspace ID (prefixed)
72: 
73: **How to Obtain:**
74: 1. Log in to ClickUp
75: 2. Go to Settings вҶ’ Apps вҶ’ API
76: 3. Generate new API key
77: 4. Copy your Team ID from workspace settings
78: 
79: **Format (Code Mode prefixed):**
80: ```bash
81: # Code Mode requires prefixed variable names: {manual_name}_{VAR}
82: clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_1234567890_ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOP
83: clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=12345678
84: ```
85: 
86: **Permissions**: Ensure API key has necessary scopes for task management
87: 
88: ### Figma
89: 
90: **Required Variables:**
91: - `figma_FIGMA_API_KEY` - Personal access token for Figma API (prefixed)
92: 
93: **How to Obtain:**
94: 1. Log in to Figma
95: 2. Go to Settings вҶ’ Account вҶ’ Personal Access Tokens
96: 3. Click "Generate new token"
97: 4. Copy token immediately (shown only once)
98: 
99: **Format (Code Mode prefixed):**
100: ```bash
101: # Code Mode requires prefixed variable names: {manual_name}_{VAR}
102: figma_FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567890
103: ```
104: 
105: **Permissions**: Token inherits your Figma account permissions
106: 
107: ### Notion
108: 
109: **Required Variables:**
110: - `notion_NOTION_TOKEN` - Integration token for Notion API (prefixed)
111: 
112: **How to Obtain:**
113: 1. Go to https://www.notion.so/my-integrations
114: 2. Click "New integration"
115: 3. Configure integration settings
116: 4. Copy the "Internal Integration Token"
117: 5. Share relevant Notion pages/databases with your integration
118: 
119: **Format (Code Mode prefixed):**
120: ```bash
121: # Code Mode requires prefixed variable names: {manual_name}_{VAR}
122: notion_NOTION_TOKEN=ntn_1234567890abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz
123: ```
124: 
125: **Important**: Must share pages/databases with integration before accessing via API
126: 
127: ### GitHub (Optional)
128: 
129: **Required Variables:**
130: - `github_GITHUB_TOKEN` or `github_GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` - Personal access token for GitHub API (prefixed)
131: 
132: **How to Obtain:**
133: 1. Log in to GitHub
134: 2. Go to Settings вҶ’ Developer settings вҶ’ Personal access tokens вҶ’ Tokens (classic)
135: 3. Click "Generate new token"
136: 4. Select scopes (repo, workflow, etc.)
137: 5. Copy token immediately
138: 
139: **Format (Code Mode prefixed):**
140: ```bash
141: # Code Mode requires prefixed variable names: {manual_name}_{VAR}
142: github_GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_1234567890abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz
143: ```
144: 
145: **Scopes**: Select based on needed permissions (read/write repos, issues, PRs, etc.)
146: 
147: ### MyService (Optional - for direct API access)
148: 
149: **Required Variables:**
150: - `myservice_MYSERVICE_API_TOKEN` - API token for MyService workspace (prefixed)
151: 
152: **How to Obtain:**
153: 1. Log in to MyService
154: 2. Go to Account Settings вҶ’ API Access
155: 3. Generate new API token
156: 4. Copy token
157: 
158: **Format (Code Mode prefixed):**
159: ```bash
160: # Code Mode requires prefixed variable names: {manual_name}_{VAR}
161: myservice_MYSERVICE_API_TOKEN=your_myservice_api_token_here
162: ```
163: 
164: **Note**: MyService remote MCP (`mcp-remote https://mcp.myservice.com/sse`) doesn't require local token
165: 
166: ### Chrome DevTools
167: 
168: **Required Variables:** None
169: 
170: **Configuration:**
171: ```bash
172: # Chrome DevTools - No authentication required
173: # Launches local Chrome instance with debugging enabled
174: ```
175: 
176: **Note**: Chrome DevTools MCP runs locally and doesn't require API credentials
177: 
178: ---
179: 
180: ## CRITICAL: Variable Naming Convention
181: 
182: ### Code Mode Prefixes All Environment Variables
183: 
184: **вҡ пёҸ IMPORTANT**: Code Mode automatically prefixes all environment variables with the `{manual_name}_` from your `.utcp_config.json` configuration.
185: 
186: **The Pattern:**
187: ```
188: {manual_name}_{VARIABLE_NAME}
189: ```
190: 
191: **Example:**
192: - Config has `"name": "clickup"` in `.utcp_config.json`
193: - Config references `${CLICKUP_API_KEY}` in the env section
194: - Your `.env` file MUST use: `clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_xxx`
195: 
196: ### Why This Matters
197: 
198: When Code Mode loads environment variables, it prefixes them with the manual name to avoid conflicts between different MCP servers that might use the same variable names.
199: 
200: **Common Error:**
201: ```
202: Error: Variable 'clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY' referenced in call template configuration not found
203: ```
204: 
205: This error occurs when your `.env` uses `CLICKUP_API_KEY` (non-prefixed) but Code Mode expects `clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY` (prefixed).
206: 
207: ### Quick Reference Table
208: 
209: | Manual Name (in config) | Config Reference | .env Variable Name |
210: |-------------------------|------------------|-------------------|
211: | `clickup` | `${CLICKUP_API_KEY}` | `clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY` |
212: | `figma` | `${FIGMA_API_KEY}` | `figma_FIGMA_API_KEY` |
213: | `notion` | `${NOTION_TOKEN}` | `notion_NOTION_TOKEN` |
214: | `github` | `${GITHUB_TOKEN}` | `github_GITHUB_TOKEN` |
215: 
216: ### Validation
217: 
218: Before starting Code Mode, verify your `.env` variables match the expected prefixed format:
219: ```bash
220: # Check what prefix your config uses
221: grep '"name":' .utcp_config.json
222: 
223: # Verify your .env has the prefixed variables
224: grep '_CLICKUP_API_KEY' .env  # Should show: clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=...
225: grep '_FIGMA_API_KEY' .env    # Should show: figma_FIGMA_API_KEY=...
226: ```
227: 
228: ---
229: 
230: ## 4. SECURITY BEST PRACTICES
231: 
232: ### .gitignore Configuration
233: 
234: **Add to `.gitignore`:**
235: 
236: ```gitignore
237: # Environment variables
238: .env
239: .env.local
240: .env.*.local
241: 
242: # Code Mode UTCP
243: .utcp_config.json
244: ```
245: 
246: **Why**: Prevents accidental commit of credentials to version control
247: 
248: ### Environment File Security
249: 
250: **DO:**
251: - вң… Use `.env` for local development only
252: - вң… Use separate `.env` files for different environments (dev, staging, prod)
253: - вң… Rotate tokens regularly (every 90 days recommended)
254: - вң… Use minimum required permissions for each token
255: - вң… Store production credentials in secure secret management (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, etc.)
256: 
257: **DON'T:**
258: - вқҢ Commit `.env` to version control
259: - вқҢ Share `.env` files via email or chat
260: - вқҢ Use production credentials in development
261: - вқҢ Grant unnecessary permissions to API tokens
262: - вқҢ Hard-code credentials in config files
263: 
264: ### Token Rotation
265: 
266: **Schedule:**
267: - Development tokens: Every 90 days
268: - Production tokens: Every 30-60 days
269: - Compromised tokens: Immediately
270: 
271: **Process:**
272: 1. Generate new token
273: 2. Update `.env` file
274: 3. Test connections
275: 4. Revoke old token
276: 5. Update documentation
277: 
278: ---
279: 
280: ## 5. CONFIGURATION REFERENCE
281: 
282: ### Variable Syntax in .utcp_config.json
283: 
284: **Correct:**
285: ```json
286: "env": {
287:   "CLICKUP_API_KEY": "${CLICKUP_API_KEY}"
288: }
289: ```
290: 
291: **Incorrect:**
292: ```json
293: "env": {
294:   "CLICKUP_API_KEY": "$CLICKUP_API_KEY"  // Missing braces
295: }
296: ```
297: 
298: **Pattern**: Always use `${VARIABLE_NAME}` for environment variable references
299: 
300: ### Loading Environment Variables
301: 
302: **Automatic loading** (configured in `.utcp_config.json`):
303: ```json
304: "load_variables_from": [
305:   {
306:     "variable_loader_type": "dotenv",
307:     "env_file_path": ".env"
308:   }
309: ]
310: ```
311: 
312: **Manual loading** (if needed):
313: ```bash
314: # Export variables manually
315: export CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your_key
316: export CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=12345
317: ```
318: 
319: ### Multiple Environment Files
320: 
321: **Use case**: Different environments (dev, staging, prod)
322: 
323: **Setup:**
324: ```bash
325: .env.development
326: .env.staging
327: .env.production
328: ```
329: 
330: **Configuration:**
331: ```json
332: "load_variables_from": [
333:   {
334:     "variable_loader_type": "dotenv",
335:     "env_file_path": ".env.development"
336:   }
337: ]
338: ```
339: 
340: **Best Practice**: Use `.env` for local development, CI/CD for other environments
341: 
342: ---
343: 
344: ## 6. TROUBLESHOOTING
345: 
346: ### Variable Not Found Error
347: 
348: **Error:**
349: ```
350: Error: Environment variable CLICKUP_API_KEY not found
351: ```
352: 
353: **Solutions:**
354: 1. Verify variable exists in `.env` file
355: 2. Check variable name spelling (case-sensitive)
356: 3. Ensure `.env` file is in correct location
357: 4. Verify `.utcp_config.json` references correct `.env` path
358: 5. Check for syntax errors in `.env` (no spaces around `=`)
359: 
360: ### Invalid Token Error
361: 
362: **Error:**
363: ```
364: Error: Authentication failed: Invalid API token
365: ```
366: 
367: **Solutions:**
368: 1. Verify token hasn't expired
369: 2. Check token has necessary permissions
370: 3. Ensure no extra whitespace in token value
371: 4. Confirm token format matches expected pattern
372: 5. Try regenerating token
373: 
374: ### Permission Denied Error
375: 
376: **Error:**
377: ```
378: Error: Access denied to resource
379: ```
380: 
381: **Solutions:**
382: 1. Verify token has required scopes/permissions
383: 2. For Notion: Ensure integration has access to pages/databases
384: 3. For GitHub: Check token scopes include needed permissions
385: 4. For Figma: Verify token has access to files/projects
386: 
387: ---
388: 
389: ## 7. TEMPLATE CUSTOMIZATION
390: 
391: ### Adding New MCP Server Credentials
392: 
393: **Example: Adding Slack MCP**
394: 
395: ```bash
396: # Slack Configuration
397: SLACK_BOT_TOKEN=xoxb-your-bot-token
398: SLACK_APP_TOKEN=xapp-your-app-token
399: SLACK_WORKSPACE_ID=your-workspace-id
400: ```
401: 
402: Then reference in `.utcp_config.json`:
403: ```json
404: {
405:   "name": "slack",
406:   "call_template_type": "mcp",
407:   "config": {
408:     "mcpServers": {
409:       "slack": {
410:         "env": {
411:           "SLACK_BOT_TOKEN": "${SLACK_BOT_TOKEN}",
412:           "SLACK_APP_TOKEN": "${SLACK_APP_TOKEN}"
413:         }
414:       }
415:     }
416:   }
417: }
418: ```
419: 
420: ### Removing Unused Credentials
421: 
422: **Steps:**
423: 1. Comment out or remove variables from `.env`
424: 2. Remove corresponding manual from `.utcp_config.json`
425: 3. Revoke token in service provider (optional but recommended)
426: 
427: **Example:**
428: ```bash
429: # Figma Configuration (removed - no longer using)
430: # FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_old_token
431: ```
432: 
433: ---
434: 
435: ## 8. VALIDATION CHECKLIST
436: 
437: **Before using Code Mode, verify:**
438: 
439: - [ ] `.env` file exists in project root
440: - [ ] All required variables are set (no placeholder values)
441: - [ ] `.env` is added to `.gitignore`
442: - [ ] Variable names match references in `.utcp_config.json`
443: - [ ] No extra whitespace around `=` in `.env`
444: - [ ] Tokens have necessary permissions
445: - [ ] Tokens are valid and not expired
446: 
447: **Test configuration:**
448: ```typescript
449: // Run this to verify environment loading
450: call_tool_chain({
451:   code: `
452:     const tools = await list_tools();
453:     console.log(\`Found \${tools.tools.length} tools\`);
454:     return { success: true, toolCount: tools.tools.length };
455:   `
456: });
457: ```
458: 
459: **Expected result**: Should return tool count without authentication errors
460: 
461: ---
462: 
463: ## 9. RELATED RESOURCES
464: 
465: ### Templates
466: - [config_template.md](./config_template.md) - Complete .utcp_config.json configuration template
467: 
468: ### Reference Files
469: - [configuration.md](../references/configuration.md) - Comprehensive configuration guide for Code Mode UTCP
470: - [naming_convention.md](../references/naming_convention.md) - Tool naming conventions and patterns for Code Mode
471: 
472: **Template Version**: 1.0.0
473: **Last Updated**: 2025-01-23
474: **Compatibility**: Code Mode UTCP v1.x

(End of file - total 474 lines)
</content>

# Environment Variables Template

Copy-ready `.env` template for Code Mode UTCP authentication with all supported MCP servers.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Centralized environment variables for Code Mode UTCP authentication and configuration. Use this template to securely manage credentials for ClickUp, Figma, Notion, GitHub, MyService, and Chrome DevTools MCP servers.

### Usage

Copy this template to `.env` in your project root, then replace placeholder values with your actual credentials. Never commit `.env` to version control - add it to `.gitignore` immediately.

display.text:
---
title: Environment Variables Template
description: Complete .env file template with placeholders for all MCP server credentials and API tokens.
---

# Environment Variables Template

Copy-ready `.env` template for Code Mode UTCP authentication with all supported MCP servers.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Centralized environment variables for Code Mode UTCP authentication and configuration. Use this template to securely manage credentials for ClickUp, Figma, Notion, GitHub, MyService, and Chrome DevTools MCP servers.

### Usage

Copy this template to `.env` in your project root, then replace placeholder values with your actual credentials. Never commit `.env` to version control - add it to `.gitignore` immediately.

---

## 2. TEMPLATE

```bash
# Code Mode UTCP - Environment Variables Template
# Copy this to .env and fill in your actual credentials
#
# вҡ пёҸ CRITICAL: Code Mode uses PREFIXED variable names!
# Format: {manual_name}_{VAR} where manual_name is from .utcp_config.json
# Example: If your config has "name": "clickup", use clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY

# ClickUp Configuration
# (Code Mode prefixes all env vars with the manual name from .utcp_config.json)
clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your_api_key_here
clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=your_team_id_here

# Figma Configuration
# (Code Mode prefixes all env vars with the manual name from .utcp_config.json)
figma_FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your_token_here

# Notion Configuration
# (Code Mode prefixes all env vars with the manual name from .utcp_config.json)
notion_NOTION_TOKEN=ntn_your_token_here

# GitHub Configuration (Optional - add GitHub MCP to .utcp_config.json if needed)
# See config_template.md Section 5 for GitHub MCP setup
# (Code Mode prefixes all env vars with the manual name from .utcp_config.json)
github_GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_your_token_here

# MyService Configuration (if using direct API, not remote MCP)
# (Code Mode prefixes all env vars with the manual name from .utcp_config.json)
myservice_MYSERVICE_API_TOKEN=your_myservice_token_here

# Chrome DevTools (usually no auth needed)
# No environment variables required

# Add additional MCP server credentials below as needed
# Remember: Always prefix with the manual name from your .utcp_config.json!
```

---

## 3. CREDENTIAL SOURCES

### ClickUp

**Required Variables:**
- `clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY` - Personal API key for ClickUp workspace (prefixed)
- `clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID` - ClickUp team/workspace ID (prefixed)

**How to Obtain:**
1. Log in to ClickUp
2. Go to Settings вҶ’ Apps вҶ’ API
3. Generate new API key
4. Copy your Team ID from workspace settings

**Format (Code Mode prefixed):**
```bash
# Code Mode requires prefixed variable names: {manual_name}_{VAR}
clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_1234567890_ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOP
clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=12345678
```

**Permissions**: Ensure API key has necessary scopes for task management

### Figma

**Required Variables:**
- `figma_FIGMA_API_KEY` - Personal access token for Figma API (prefixed)

**How to Obtain:**
1. Log in to Figma
2. Go to Settings вҶ’ Account вҶ’ Personal Access Tokens
3. Click "Generate new token"
4. Copy token immediately (shown only once)

**Format (Code Mode prefixed):**
```bash
# Code Mode requires prefixed variable names: {manual_name}_{VAR}
figma_FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567890
```

**Permissions**: Token inherits your Figma account permissions

### Notion

**Required Variables:**
- `notion_NOTION_TOKEN` - Integration token for Notion API (prefixed)

**How to Obtain:**
1. Go to https://www.notion.so/my-integrations
2. Click "New integration"
3. Configure integration settings
4. Copy the "Internal Integration Token"
5. Share relevant Notion pages/databases with your integration

**Format (Code Mode prefixed):**
```bash
# Code Mode requires prefixed variable names: {manual_name}_{VAR}
notion_NOTION_TOKEN=ntn_1234567890abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz
```

**Important**: Must share pages/databases with integration before accessing via API

### GitHub (Optional)

**Required Variables:**
- `github_GITHUB_TOKEN` or `github_GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` - Personal access token for GitHub API (prefixed)

**How to Obtain:**
1. Log in to GitHub
2. Go to Settings вҶ’ Developer settings вҶ’ Personal access tokens вҶ’ Tokens (classic)
3. Click "Generate new token"
4. Select scopes (repo, workflow, etc.)
5. Copy token immediately

**Format (Code Mode prefixed):**
```bash
# Code Mode requires prefixed variable names: {manual_name}_{VAR}
github_GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_1234567890abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz
```

**Scopes**: Select based on needed permissions (read/write repos, issues, PRs, etc.)

### MyService (Optional - for direct API access)

**Required Variables:**
- `myservice_MYSERVICE_API_TOKEN` - API token for MyService workspace (prefixed)

**How to Obtain:**
1. Log in to MyService
2. Go to Account Settings вҶ’ API Access
3. Generate new API token
4. Copy token

**Format (Code Mode prefixed):**
```bash
# Code Mode requires prefixed variable names: {manual_name}_{VAR}
myservice_MYSERVICE_API_TOKEN=your_myservice_api_token_here
```

**Note**: MyService remote MCP (`mcp-remote https://mcp.myservice.com/sse`) doesn't require local token

### Chrome DevTools

**Required Variables:** None

**Configuration:**
```bash
# Chrome DevTools - No authentication required
# Launches local Chrome instance with debugging enabled
```

**Note**: Chrome DevTools MCP runs locally and doesn't require API credentials

---

## CRITICAL: Variable Naming Convention

### Code Mode Prefixes All Environment Variables

**вҡ пёҸ IMPORTANT**: Code Mode automatically prefixes all environment variables with the `{manual_name}_` from your `.utcp_config.json` configuration.

**The Pattern:**
```
{manual_name}_{VARIABLE_NAME}
```

**Example:**
- Config has `"name": "clickup"` in `.utcp_config.json`
- Config references `${CLICKUP_API_KEY}` in the env section
- Your `.env` file MUST use: `clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_xxx`

### Why This Matters

When Code Mode loads environment variables, it prefixes them with the manual name to avoid conflicts between different MCP servers that might use the same variable names.

**Common Error:**
```
Error: Variable 'clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY' referenced in call template configuration not found
```

This error occurs when your `.env` uses `CLICKUP_API_KEY` (non-prefixed) but Code Mode expects `clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY` (prefixed).

### Quick Reference Table

| Manual Name (in config) | Config Reference | .env Variable Name |
|-------------------------|------------------|-------------------|
| `clickup` | `${CLICKUP_API_KEY}` | `clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY` |
| `figma` | `${FIGMA_API_KEY}` | `figma_FIGMA_API_KEY` |
| `notion` | `${NOTION_TOKEN}` | `notion_NOTION_TOKEN` |
| `github` | `${GITHUB_TOKEN}` | `github_GITHUB_TOKEN` |

### Validation

Before starting Code Mode, verify your `.env` variables match the expected prefixed format:
```bash
# Check what prefix your config uses
grep '"name":' .utcp_config.json

# Verify your .env has the prefixed variables
grep '_CLICKUP_API_KEY' .env  # Should show: clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=...
grep '_FIGMA_API_KEY' .env    # Should show: figma_FIGMA_API_KEY=...
```

---

## 4. SECURITY BEST PRACTICES

### .gitignore Configuration

**Add to `.gitignore`:**

```gitignore
# Environment variables
.env
.env.local
.env.*.local

# Code Mode UTCP
.utcp_config.json
```

**Why**: Prevents accidental commit of credentials to version control

### Environment File Security

**DO:**
- вң… Use `.env` for local development only
- вң… Use separate `.env` files for different environments (dev, staging, prod)
- вң… Rotate tokens regularly (every 90 days recommended)
- вң… Use minimum required permissions for each token
- вң… Store production credentials in secure secret management (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, etc.)

**DON'T:**
- вқҢ Commit `.env` to version control
- вқҢ Share `.env` files via email or chat
- вқҢ Use production credentials in development
- вқҢ Grant unnecessary permissions to API tokens
- вқҢ Hard-code credentials in config files

### Token Rotation

**Schedule:**
- Development tokens: Every 90 days
- Production tokens: Every 30-60 days
- Compromised tokens: Immediately

**Process:**
1. Generate new token
2. Update `.env` file
3. Test connections
4. Revoke old token
5. Update documentation

---

## 5. CONFIGURATION REFERENCE

### Variable Syntax in .utcp_config.json

**Correct:**
```json
"env": {
  "CLICKUP_API_KEY": "${CLICKUP_API_KEY}"
}
```

**Incorrect:**
```json
"env": {
  "CLICKUP_API_KEY": "$CLICKUP_API_KEY"  // Missing braces
}
```

**Pattern**: Always use `${VARIABLE_NAME}` for environment variable references

### Loading Environment Variables

**Automatic loading** (configured in `.utcp_config.json`):
```json
"load_variables_from": [
  {
    "variable_loader_type": "dotenv",
    "env_file_path": ".env"
  }
]
```

**Manual loading** (if needed):
```bash
# Export variables manually
export CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your_key
export CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=12345
```

### Multiple Environment Files

**Use case**: Different environments (dev, staging, prod)

**Setup:**
```bash
.env.development
.env.staging
.env.production
```

**Configuration:**
```json
"load_variables_from": [
  {
    "variable_loader_type": "dotenv",
    "env_file_path": ".env.development"
  }
]
```

**Best Practice**: Use `.env` for local development, CI/CD for other environments

---

## 6. TROUBLESHOOTING

### Variable Not Found Error

**Error:**
```
Error: Environment variable CLICKUP_API_KEY not found
```

**Solutions:**
1. Verify variable exists in `.env` file
2. Check variable name spelling (case-sensitive)
3. Ensure `.env` file is in correct location
4. Verify `.utcp_config.json` references correct `.env` path
5. Check for syntax errors in `.env` (no spaces around `=`)

### Invalid Token Error

**Error:**
```
Error: Authentication failed: Invalid API token
```

**Solutions:**
1. Verify token hasn't expired
2. Check token has necessary permissions
3. Ensure no extra whitespace in token value
4. Confirm token format matches expected pattern
5. Try regenerating token

### Permission Denied Error

**Error:**
```
Error: Access denied to resource
```

**Solutions:**
1. Verify token has required scopes/permissions
2. For Notion: Ensure integration has access to pages/databases
3. For GitHub: Check token scopes include needed permissions
4. For Figma: Verify token has access to files/projects

---

## 7. TEMPLATE CUSTOMIZATION

### Adding New MCP Server Credentials

**Example: Adding Slack MCP**

```bash
# Slack Configuration
SLACK_BOT_TOKEN=xoxb-your-bot-token
SLACK_APP_TOKEN=xapp-your-app-token
SLACK_WORKSPACE_ID=your-workspace-id
```

Then reference in `.utcp_config.json`:
```json
{
  "name": "slack",
  "call_template_type": "mcp",
  "config": {
    "mcpServers": {
      "slack": {
        "env": {
          "SLACK_BOT_TOKEN": "${SLACK_BOT_TOKEN}",
          "SLACK_APP_TOKEN": "${SLACK_APP_TOKEN}"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Removing Unused Credentials

**Steps:**
1. Comment out or remove variables from `.env`
2. Remove corresponding manual from `.utcp_config.json`
3. Revoke token in service provider (optional but recommended)

**Example:**
```bash
# Figma Configuration (removed - no longer using)
# FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_old_token
```

---

## 8. VALIDATION CHECKLIST

**Before using Code Mode, verify:**

- [ ] `.env` file exists in project root
- [ ] All required variables are set (no placeholder values)
- [ ] `.env` is added to `.gitignore`
- [ ] Variable names match references in `.utcp_config.json`
- [ ] No extra whitespace around `=` in `.env`
- [ ] Tokens have necessary permissions
- [ ] Tokens are valid and not expired

**Test configuration:**
```typescript
// Run this to verify environment loading
call_tool_chain({
  code: `
    const tools = await list_tools();
    console.log(\`Found \${tools.tools.length} tools\`);
    return { success: true, toolCount: tools.tools.length };
  `
});
```

**Expected result**: Should return tool count without authentication errors

---

## 9. RELATED RESOURCES

### Templates
- [config_template.md](./config_template.md) - Complete .utcp_config.json configuration template

### Reference Files
- [configuration.md](../references/configuration.md) - Comprehensive configuration guide for Code Mode UTCP
- [naming_convention.md](../references/naming_convention.md) - Tool naming conventions and patterns for Code Mode

**Template Version**: 1.0.0
**Last Updated**: 2025-01-23
**Compatibility**: Code Mode UTCP v1.x

part.text:
# mcp-code-mode Fact-Verification Report

## 1. EXACT INVOCATION

**Four meta-tools (SKILL.md:4, allowed-tools list):**
- `mcp__code_mode__search_tools` вҖ” find tools by natural language description
- `mcp__code_mode__list_tools` вҖ” return all registered tool names
- `mcp__code_mode__tool_info` вҖ” return TypeScript interface for a tool
- `mcp__code_mode__call_tool_chain` вҖ” execute TypeScript code with tool access

**`call_tool_chain` parameters (README.md:111вҖ“117):**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `code` | string | Yes | вҖ” | TypeScript code to execute |
| `timeout` | number | No | 30000 | Execution timeout in ms |
| `max_output_size` | number | No | 200000 | Max response size in characters |

**Documented `call_tool_chain` forms (SKILL.md:48, 259):**
```typescript
call_tool_chain({ code: "await clickup.clickup_create_task({...})" })
```
SKILL.md:259 states the basic workflow as: "Execute `call_tool_chain()` with `{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}` calls."

**Tool-naming convention (SKILL.md:218вҖ“219):**
```typescript
{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}
```
Dotted namespace (`manual.tool`) is **wrong**; underscore join (`manual_manual_tool`) is correct for the second segment. `list_tools()` returns `a.b.c` format; calling uses `a.a_b_c()` format (SKILL.md:253).

**Configuration files (SKILL.md:279вҖ“280):**
- `.utcp_config.json` вҖ” project root, defines external MCP servers via `manual_call_templates[]`
- `.env` вҖ” project root, stores API keys with `{manual_name}_` prefix

## 2. CAPABILITY ROSTER

**Progressive-disclosure workflow (SKILL.md:257вҖ“261, exact order):**
1. Discover tools with `search_tools()` or `list_tools()`
2. Confirm exact callable syntax with `tool_info()`
3. Execute `call_tool_chain()` with `{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}` calls
4. Return structured state from the TypeScript block

**Context-reduction figure (SKILL.md:26):**

> "State persistence вҖ” Data flows naturally between operations"

**Error handling (SKILL.md:371вҖ“375):**
- `try/catch` prevents one failing tool from crashing the workflow
- `Promise.allSettled()` for partial-success tolerance
- `Promise.all()` when all operations must succeed

**Additional capabilities (SKILL.md:27вҖ“30):**
- 60% faster execution вҖ” single execution vs 15+ API round trips
- Type safety вҖ” full TypeScript support
- Progressive loading вҖ” tools discovered on-demand, zero upfront cost

## 5. TROUBLESHOOTING & FAQ

**Concrete failure modes:**

| Failure | Cause | Fix | Source |
|---------|-------|-----|--------|
| `"Tool is not a function"` | Missing manual prefix: `myservice.sites_list` instead of `myservice.myservice_sites_list` | Use `search_tools()` to discover exact name | README.md:319вҖ“325 |
| `"Variable 'clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY' not found"` | `.env` uses unprefixed key name | Prepend `{manual_name}_` to key in `.env` | README.md:329вҖ“335 |
| `"Execution timeout"` | Workflow calls >2 tools on default 30s | Increase `timeout`: 60s for 3вҖ“5 tools, 120s+ for 6+ | README.md:339вҖ“345 |
| `"UTCP config file not found"` | `UTCP_CONFIG_FILE` points to relative path or file missing | Use absolute path, verify file exists | README.md:349вҖ“355 |
| `TypeError: myservice.myservice is not a function` | Double dot `manual.manual.tool` instead of `manual.manual_tool` | Use single dot: `manual.manual_tool` | naming_convention.md:73вҖ“78 |
| `Tool not found: myservice.myserviceSitesList` | camelCase instead of snake_case | Use snake_case: `myservice_sites_list` | naming_convention.md:94вҖ“98 |

**Top 3вҖ“5 user questions (README.md:359вҖ“375):**

1. **When should I NOT use call_tool_chain?** вҖ” Not for file ops (Read/Write/Edit/Grep/Glob/Bash), native MCP tools (Spec Kit Memory, Sequential Thinking, Code Graph), or first-class app connectors. Only for `.utcp_config.json` tools.
2. **Why does list_tools() not show Sequential Thinking?** вҖ” Those are native MCP tools in `opencode.json`, not `.utcp_config.json`. Discovery tools only see Code Mode tools.
3. **Can I add a new MCP server without restarting?** вҖ” Yes, use `register_manual` at runtime. For persistent registration, add to `.utcp_config.json` and restart.
4. **What happens if one tool fails in a multi-step workflow?** вҖ” Without try/catch, the entire execution stops. Use `try/catch` and `Promise.allSettled()` for resilience.

1. **Manual count and composition (README.md:14):**
   README claims "currently 5 manuals: 2 Chrome DevTools instances, ClickUp, Figma, GitHub". INSTALL_GUIDE.md:68вҖ“76 lists **6 manuals** (github, figma, chrome_devtools_1, chrome_devtools_2, clickup, **myservice**) with 159 tools total. **MyService is missing from README's count.**

2. **Tool count (README.md:178):**
   `references/tool_catalog.md` is described as "currently 5 in .utcp_config.json". INSTALL_GUIDE.md:76 says **6 manuals**. tool_catalog.md:8 says "8 MCP servers" including ShadCN UI, Imagician, and Video Audio. The "5" is stale.

3. **Version inconsistency:**
   SKILL.md:5 frontmatter says `version: 1.0.7.0`. INSTALL_GUIDE.md:5 says "Version: 2.0.0". mcp_server/package.json says `version: 1.0.9`. README.md:149 says "Version: 1.0.7.0". Three different version numbers across files.

4. **Native Tools count (README.md:99):**
   README says "Native Tools (7 total)" listing `call_tool_chain`, `search_tools`, `list_tools`, `tool_info`, `get_required_keys_for_tool`, `register_manual`, `deregister_manual`. SKILL.md:4 allowed-tools lists exactly **4** tools. The architecture.md:28вҖ“33 shows only **3** tools in the AI-visible layer. The "7" includes `get_required_keys_for_tool`, `register_manual`, and `deregister_manual` which are not in the SKILL.md allowed-tools list.

5. **Context-reduction figure qualifier (README.md:150):**
   README says "98.7% reduction" for "47 tools" at "141k" tokens. SKILL.md:26 says the same. INSTALL_GUIDE.md:48 says "unified access to 159 MCP tools" but the 1.6k figure still applies. The "47 tools" baseline is accurate per SKILL.md but the INSTALL_GUIDE.md references 159 tools at the same 1.6k cost, which could confuse readers.

6. **Execution speed claim (README.md:151):**
   README says "85% faster" for execution time. SKILL.md:27 says "60% faster execution". INSTALL_GUIDE.md:1453 says "85% faster". The SKILL.md (authoritative agent instructions) and README disagree: **60% vs 85%**.

7. **tool_catalog.md description (README.md:393):**

The actual tool_catalog.md:3вҖ“8 says "Reference catalog of 170+ tools across 7 MCP servers" (later lists 8 servers including ShadCN UI, Imagician, Video Audio). Neither the "5" nor the "7"/"8" matches INSTALL_GUIDE.md's "6 manuals, 159 tools".

8. **workflows.md description (README.md:395):**
   README says "Five end-to-end workflow examples". workflows.md actually documents **6 patterns** (5 core + parallel execution patterns in В§7). The "five" is stale вҖ” В§7 adds a sixth parallel-execution pattern set.

9. **Config example manual name (README.md:196):**
   README uses `"name": "clickup_official"` in its config example. INSTALL_GUIDE.md:529 uses `"name": "clickup"`. The naming convention docs all use `clickup` as the canonical example. The `clickup_official` name in README is inconsistent with every other file.
